    93	def reassign_root_page_locale_on_delete(sender, instance, **kwargs):
    94	    # if we're deleting the locale used on the root page node, reassign that to a new locale first
    95	    root_page_with_this_locale = Page.objects.filter(depth=1, locale=instance)
    96	    if root_page_with_this_locale.exists():
    97	        # Select the default locale, if one exists and isn't the one being deleted
    98	        try:
    99	            new_locale = Locale.get_default()
   100	            default_locale_is_ok = new_locale != instance
   101	        except (Locale.DoesNotExist, LookupError):
   102	            default_locale_is_ok = False
   103	
   104	        if not default_locale_is_ok:
   105	            # fall back on any remaining locale
   106	            new_locale = Locale.all_objects.exclude(pk=instance.pk).first()
   107	
   108	        root_page_with_this_locale.update(locale=new_locale)

   121	def get_page_content_types(include_base_page_type=True):
   122	    """

   144	class BasePageManager(MP_NodeManager):
   145	    def get_queryset(self):
   146	        return self._queryset_class(self.model).order_by("path")
   147	
   148	    def get_by_natural_key(self, url_path):
   149	        """Get page by URL"""
   150	        return self.get(url_path=url_path)

   152	    def first_common_ancestor_of(self, pages, include_self=False, strict=False):
   153	        """
   154	        This is similar to ``PageQuerySet.first_common_ancestor`` but works
   155	        for a list of pages instead of a queryset.
   156	        """
   157	        if not pages:
   158	            if strict:
   159	                raise self.model.DoesNotExist("Can not find ancestor of empty list")
   160	            return self.model.get_first_root_node()
   161	
   162	        if include_self:
   163	            paths = list({page.path for page in pages})
   164	        else:
   165	            paths = list({page.path[: -self.model.steplen] for page in pages})
   166	
   167	        # This method works on anything, not just file system paths.
   168	        common_parent_path = posixpath.commonprefix(paths)
   169	        extra_chars = len(common_parent_path) % self.model.steplen
   170	        if extra_chars != 0:
   171	            common_parent_path = common_parent_path[:-extra_chars]
   172	
   173	        if common_parent_path == "":
   174	            if strict:
   175	                raise self.model.DoesNotExist("No common ancestor found!")
   176	
   177	            return self.model.get_first_root_node()
   178	
   179	        return self.get(path=common_parent_path)

   181	    def annotate_parent_page(self, pages):
   182	        """
   183	        Annotates each page with its parent page. This is implemented as a
   184	        manager-only method instead of a QuerySet method so it can be used with
   185	        search results.
   186	
   187	        If given a QuerySet, this method will evaluate it. Only use this method
   188	        when you are ready to consume the queryset, e.g. after pagination has
   189	        been applied. This is typically done in the view's ``get_context_data``
   190	        using ``context["object_list"]``.
   191	
   192	        This method does not return a new queryset, but modifies the existing one,
   193	        to ensure any references to the queryset in the view's context are updated
   194	        (e.g. when using ``context_object_name``).
   195	        """
   196	        parent_page_paths = {
   197	            Page._get_parent_path_from_path(page.path) for page in pages
   198	        }
   199	        parent_pages_by_path = {
   200	            page.path: page
   201	            for page in Page.objects.filter(path__in=parent_page_paths).specific(
   202	                defer=True
   203	            )
   204	        }
   205	        for page in pages:
   206	            parent_page = parent_pages_by_path.get(
   207	                Page._get_parent_path_from_path(page.path)
   208	            )
   209	            page._parent_page = parent_page

   498	    @staticmethod
   499	    def route_for_request(request: HttpRequest, path: str) -> RouteResult | None:
   500	        """
   501	        Find the page route for the given HTTP request object, and URL path. The route
   502	        result (`page`, `args`, and `kwargs`) will be cached via
   503	        ``request._wagtail_route_for_request``.
   504	        """
   505	        if not hasattr(request, "_wagtail_route_for_request"):
   506	            try:
   507	                # we need a valid Site object for this request in order to proceed
   508	                if site := Site.find_for_request(request):
   509	                    path_components = [
   510	                        component for component in path.split("/") if component
   511	                    ]
   512	                    request._wagtail_route_for_request = (
   513	                        site.root_page.localized.specific.route(
   514	                            request, path_components
   515	                        )
   516	                    )
   517	                else:
   518	                    request._wagtail_route_for_request = None
   519	            except Http404:
   520	                # .route() can raise Http404
   521	                request._wagtail_route_for_request = None
   522	
   523	        return request._wagtail_route_for_request

   525	    @staticmethod
   526	    def find_for_request(request: HttpRequest, path: str) -> Page | None:
   527	        """
   528	        Find the page for the given HTTP request object, and URL path. The full
   529	        page route will be cached via ``request._wagtail_route_for_request``.
   530	        """
   531	        result = Page.route_for_request(request, path)
   532	        if result is not None:
   533	            return result[0]

   577	    def set_url_path(self, parent):
   578	        """
   579	        Populate the url_path field based on this page's slug and the specified parent page.
   580	        (We pass a parent in here, rather than retrieving it via get_parent, so that we can give
   581	        new unsaved pages a meaningful URL when previewing them; at that point the page has not
   582	        been assigned a position in the tree, as far as treebeard is concerned.
   583	        """
   584	        if parent:
   585	            self.url_path = parent.url_path + self.slug + "/"
   586	        else:
   587	            # a page without a parent is the tree root, which always has a url_path of '/'
   588	            self.url_path = "/"
   589	
   590	        return self.url_path

   592	    @staticmethod
   593	    def _slug_is_available(slug, parent_page, page=None):
   594	        """
   595	        Determine whether the given slug is available for use on a child page of
   596	        parent_page. If 'page' is passed, the slug is intended for use on that page
   597	        (and so it will be excluded from the duplicate check).
   598	        """
   599	        if parent_page is None:
   600	            # the root page's slug can be whatever it likes...
   601	            return True
   602	
   603	        siblings = parent_page.get_children()
   604	        if page:
   605	            siblings = siblings.not_page(page)
   606	
   607	        return not siblings.filter(slug=slug).exists()

   609	    def _get_autogenerated_slug(self, base_slug):
   610	        candidate_slug = base_slug
   611	        suffix = 1
   612	        parent_page = self.get_parent()
   613	
   614	        while not Page._slug_is_available(candidate_slug, parent_page, self):
   615	            # try with incrementing suffix until we find a slug which is available
   616	            suffix += 1
   617	            candidate_slug = "%s-%d" % (base_slug, suffix)
   618	
   619	        return candidate_slug

   625	        This will be called just before the initial save.

   627	        parent = self.get_parent()
   628	        if parent is not None:
   629	            return (
   630	                parent.specific_class.objects.defer()
   631	                .select_related("locale")
   632	                .get(id=parent.id)
   633	                .locale
   634	            )
   635	
   636	        return super().get_default_locale()

   645	    def _set_core_field_defaults(self):
   646	        """
   647	        Set default values for core fields (slug, draft_title, locale) that need to be
   648	        in place before validating or saving
   649	        """
   650	
   651	        # If path is unset, then this cleaning step must be happening in advance of the
   652	        # final save, and there is no point trying to auto-generate a slug yet, as we
   653	        # don't know the set of sibling pages to de-duplicate against. In this case,
   654	        # this method will be called again as part of the call to `save()` after the
   655	        # path is populated, and we will auto-generate the slug at that point if
   656	        # necessary.
   657	        if (not self.slug) and self.path:
   658	            # Try to auto-populate slug from title
   659	            allow_unicode = getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_ALLOW_UNICODE_SLUGS", True)
   660	            base_slug = slugify(self.title, allow_unicode=allow_unicode)
   661	
   662	            # only proceed if we get a non-empty base slug back from slugify
   663	            if base_slug:
   664	                self.slug = self._get_autogenerated_slug(base_slug)
   665	
   666	        if not self.draft_title:
   667	            self.draft_title = self.title
   668	
   669	        # Set the locale
   670	        if self.locale_id is None:
   671	            self.locale = self.get_default_locale()

   673	    def full_clean(self, *args, **kwargs):
   674	        self._set_core_field_defaults()

   677	    def _check_slug_is_unique(self):
   678	        parent_page = self.get_parent()
   679	        if not Page._slug_is_available(self.slug, parent_page, self):
   680	            raise ValidationError(
   681	                {
   682	                    "slug": _(
   683	                        "The slug '%(page_slug)s' is already in use within the parent page at '%(parent_url_path)s'."
   684	                    )
   685	                    % {"page_slug": self.slug, "parent_url_path": parent_page.url}
   686	                }
   687	            )
   688	
   689	    def clean(self):
   690	        super().clean()
   691	        self._check_slug_is_unique()

   693	    def minimal_clean(self):
   694	        self._set_core_field_defaults()
   695	        self.title = self._meta.get_field("title").clean(self.title, self)
   696	        self._check_slug_is_unique()

   698	    def is_site_root(self):
   699	        """
   700	        Returns True if this page is the root of any site.
   701	
   702	        This includes translations of site root pages as well.
   703	        """
   704	        # `_is_site_root` may be populated by `annotate_site_root_state` on `PageQuerySet` as a
   705	        # performance optimisation
   706	        if hasattr(self, "_is_site_root"):
   707	            return self._is_site_root
   708	
   709	        return Site.objects.filter(
   710	            root_page__translation_key=self.translation_key
   711	        ).exists()

   713	    @transaction.atomic
   714	    # ensure that changes are only committed when we have updated all descendant URL paths, to preserve consistency
   715	    def save(self, clean=True, user=None, log_action=False, **kwargs):
   716	        """
   717	        Writes the page to the database, performing additional housekeeping tasks to ensure data
   718	        integrity:
   719	
   720	        * ``locale``, ``draft_title`` and ``slug`` are set to default values if not provided, with ``slug``
   721	          being generated from the title with a suffix to ensure uniqueness within the parent page
   722	          where necessary
   723	        * The ``url_path`` field is set based on the ``slug`` and the parent page
   724	        * If the ``slug`` has changed, the ``url_path`` of this page and all descendants is updated and
   725	          a :ref:`page_slug_changed` signal is sent
   726	
   727	        New pages should be saved by passing the unsaved page instance to the
   728	        :meth:`~treebeard.mp_tree.MP_Node.add_child`
   729	        or :meth:`~treebeard.mp_tree.MP_Node.add_sibling` method of an existing page, which will correctly update
   730	        the fields responsible for tracking the page's location in the tree.
   731	
   732	        If ``clean=False`` is passed, the page is saved without validation. This is appropriate for updates that only
   733	        change metadata such as `latest_revision` while keeping content and page location unchanged.
   734	
   735	        If ``clean=True`` is passed (the default), and the page has ``live=True`` set, the page is validated using
   736	        :meth:`~django.db.models.Model.full_clean` before saving.
   737	
   738	        If ``clean=True`` is passed, and the page has ``live=False`` set, only the title and slug fields are validated.
   739	        """
   740	        if clean:
   741	            if self.live:
   742	                self.full_clean()
   743	            else:
   744	                # Saving as draft; only perform the minimal validation to satisfy data integrity
   745	                self.minimal_clean()
   746	
   747	        slug_changed = False
   748	        is_new = self.id is None
   749	
   750	        if is_new:
   751	            # we are creating a record. If we're doing things properly, this should happen
   752	            # through a treebeard method like add_child, in which case the 'path' field
   753	            # has been set and so we can safely call get_parent
   754	            self.set_url_path(self.get_parent())
   755	        else:
   756	            # Check that we are committing the slug to the database
   757	            # Basically: If update_fields has been specified, and slug is not included, skip this step
   758	            if not (
   759	                "update_fields" in kwargs
   760	                and kwargs["update_fields"] is not None
   761	                and "slug" not in kwargs["update_fields"]
   762	            ):
   763	                # see if the slug has changed from the record in the db, in which case we need to
   764	                # update url_path of self and all descendants. Even though we might not need it,
   765	                # the specific page is fetched here for sending to the 'page_slug_changed' signal.
   766	                old_record = Page.objects.get(id=self.id).specific
   767	                if old_record.slug != self.slug:
   768	                    self.set_url_path(self.get_parent())
   769	                    slug_changed = True
   770	                    old_url_path = old_record.url_path
   771	                    new_url_path = self.url_path
   772	
   773	        result = super().save(**kwargs)
   774	
   775	        if slug_changed:
   776	            self._update_descendant_url_paths(old_url_path, new_url_path)
   777	            # Emit page_slug_changed signal on successful db commit
   778	            transaction.on_commit(
   779	                lambda: page_slug_changed.send(
   780	                    sender=self.specific_class or self.__class__,
   781	                    instance=self.specific,
   782	                    instance_before=old_record,
   783	                )
   784	            )
   785	
   786	        # Check if this is a root page of any sites and clear the 'wagtail_site_root_paths' key if so
   787	        # Note: New translations of existing site roots are considered site roots as well, so we must
   788	        # always check if this page is a site root, even if it's new.
   789	        if self.is_site_root():
   790	            Site.clear_site_root_paths_cache()
   791	
   792	        # Log
   793	        if is_new:
   794	            cls = type(self)
   795	            logger.info(
   796	                'Page created: "%s" id=%d content_type=%s.%s path=%s',
   797	                self.title,
   798	                self.id,
   799	                cls._meta.app_label,
   800	                cls.__name__,
   801	                self.url_path,
   802	            )
   803	
   804	        if log_action is not None:
   805	            # The default for log_action is False. i.e. don't log unless specifically instructed
   806	            # Page creation is a special case that we want logged by default, but allow skipping it
   807	            # explicitly by passing log_action=None
   808	            if is_new:
   809	                log(
   810	                    instance=self,
   811	                    action="wagtail.create",
   812	                    user=user or self.owner,
   813	                    content_changed=True,
   814	                )
   815	            elif log_action:
   816	                log(instance=self, action=log_action, user=user)
   817	
   818	        return result

   824	    @classmethod
   825	    def check(cls, **kwargs):
   826	        errors = super().check(**kwargs)
   827	
   828	        # Check that foreign keys from pages are not configured to cascade
   829	        # This is the default Django behaviour which must be explicitly overridden
   830	        # to prevent pages disappearing unexpectedly and the tree being corrupted
   831	
   832	        # get names of foreign keys pointing to parent classes (such as page_ptr)
   833	        field_exceptions = [
   834	            field.name
   835	            for model in [cls] + list(cls._meta.get_parent_list())
   836	            for field in model._meta.parents.values()
   837	            if field
   838	        ]
   839	
   840	        for field in cls._meta.fields:
   841	            if (
   842	                isinstance(field, models.ForeignKey)
   843	                and field.name not in field_exceptions
   844	            ):
   845	                if field.remote_field.on_delete == models.CASCADE:
   846	                    errors.append(
   847	                        checks.Warning(
   848	                            "Field hasn't specified on_delete action",
   849	                            hint="Set on_delete=models.SET_NULL and make sure the field is nullable or set on_delete=models.PROTECT. Wagtail does not allow simple database CASCADE because it will corrupt its tree storage.",
   850	                            obj=field,
   851	                            id="wagtailcore.W001",
   852	                        )
   853	                    )
   854	
   855	        if not isinstance(cls.objects, PageManager):
   856	            errors.append(
   857	                checks.Error(
   858	                    "Manager does not inherit from PageManager",
   859	                    hint="Ensure that custom Page managers inherit from wagtail.models.PageManager",
   860	                    obj=cls,
   861	                    id="wagtailcore.E002",
   862	                )
   863	            )
   864	
   865	        try:
   866	            cls.clean_subpage_models()
   867	        except (ValueError, LookupError) as e:
   868	            errors.append(
   869	                checks.Error(
   870	                    "Invalid subpage_types setting for %s" % cls,
   871	                    hint=str(e),
   872	                    id="wagtailcore.E002",
   873	                )
   874	            )
   875	
   876	        try:
   877	            cls.clean_parent_page_models()
   878	        except (ValueError, LookupError) as e:
   879	            errors.append(
   880	                checks.Error(
   881	                    "Invalid parent_page_types setting for %s" % cls,
   882	                    hint=str(e),
   883	                    id="wagtailcore.E002",
   884	                )
   885	            )
   886	
   887	        return errors

   889	    def _update_descendant_url_paths(self, old_url_path, new_url_path):
   890	        (
   891	            Page.objects.filter(path__startswith=self.path)
   892	            .exclude(pk=self.pk)
   893	            .update(
   894	                url_path=Concat(
   895	                    Value(new_url_path), Substr("url_path", len(old_url_path) + 1)
   896	                )
   897	            )
   898	        )

   903	        A human-readable version of this page's type.

   906	            return ""
   907	        else:
   908	            return self.specific_class.get_verbose_name()

   910	    def route(self, request, path_components):
   911	        if path_components:
   912	            # request is for a child of this page
   913	            child_slug = path_components[0]
   914	            remaining_components = path_components[1:]
   915	
   916	            try:
   917	                subpage = self.get_children().get(slug=child_slug)
   918	                # Cache the parent page on the subpage to avoid another db query
   919	                # Treebeard's get_parent will use the `_cached_parent_obj` attribute if it exists
   920	                # And update = False
   921	                subpage._cached_parent_obj = self
   922	
   923	            except Page.DoesNotExist as e:
   924	                raise Http404 from e
   925	
   926	            return subpage.specific.route(request, remaining_components)
   927	
   928	        else:
   929	            # request is for this very page
   930	            if self.live:
   931	                return RouteResult(self)
   932	            else:
   933	                raise Http404

   935	    def get_admin_display_title(self):
   936	        """
   937	        Return the title for this page as it should appear in the admin backend;
   938	        override this if you wish to display extra contextual information about the page,
   939	        such as language. By default, returns ``draft_title``.
   940	        """
   941	        # Fall back on title if draft_title is blank (which may happen if the page was created
   942	        # in a fixture or migration that didn't explicitly handle draft_title)
   943	        return self.draft_title or self.title

   945	    @transaction.atomic
   946	    def save_revision(
   947	        self,
   948	        user=None,
   949	        approved_go_live_at=None,

   951	        log_action=False,

   953	        clean=True,
   954	        overwrite_revision=None,

   956	        # Raise error if this is not the specific version of the page
   957	        if not isinstance(self, self.specific_class):
   958	            raise RuntimeError(
   959	                "page.save_revision() must be called on the specific version of the page. "
   960	                "Call page.specific.save_revision() instead."

   963	        # Raise an error if this page is an alias.

   967	                "Revisions are not required for alias pages as they are an exact copy of another page."

    25	    def descendant_of_q(self, other, inclusive=False):
    26	        q = Q(path__startswith=other.path) & Q(depth__gte=other.depth)
    27	
    28	        if not inclusive:
    29	            q &= ~Q(pk=other.pk)
    30	
    31	        return q

    36

    38	        """
    39	        return self.filter(self.descendant_of_q(other, inclusive))
    40	
    41	    def not_descendant_of(self, other, inclusive=False):
    42	        """

    44	
    45	        If inclusive is set to True, it will also exclude the specified page.
    46	        """
    47	        return self.exclude(self.descendant_of_q(other, inclusive))
    48	
    49	    def child_of_q(self, other):
    50	        return self.descendant_of_q(other) & Q(depth=other.depth + 1)
    51	
    52	    def child_of(self, other):

    55	        """

    61	        """
    62	        return self.exclude(self.child_of_q(other))
    63	
    64	    def ancestor_of_q(self, other, inclusive=False):
    65	        paths = [
    66	            other.path[0:pos]
    67	            for pos in range(0, len(other.path) + 1, other.steplen)[1:]
    68	        ]
    69	        q = Q(path__in=paths)
    70	
    71	        if not inclusive:
    72	            q &= ~Q(pk=other.pk)
    73	
    74	        return q

    88	        If inclusive is set to True, it will also exclude the specified page.

   107	    def sibling_of_q(self, other, inclusive=True):

   109	            depth=other.depth
   110	        )

   152	        clone._specific_prefetch_related_lookups = (

   182	    def select_related(self, *fields, for_specific_subqueries: bool = False):
   183	        """
   184	        Overrides Django's native :meth:`~django.db.models.query.QuerySet.select_related`
   185	        to allow related objects to be fetched by the subqueries made when a specific
   186	        queryset is evaluated.
   187	
   188	        When ``for_specific_subqueries`` is ``False`` (the default), the method functions
   189	        exactly like the original method. However, when ``True``, ``fields`` are
   190	        **required**, and must match names of ForeignKey fields on all specific models
   191	        that might be included in the result (which can include fields inherited from
   192	        concrete parents). Unlike when ``for_specific_subqueries`` is ``False``, no
   193	        validation is applied to ``fields`` when the method is called. Rather, that when
   194	        the method is called. Instead, that validation is applied for each individual
   195	        subquery when the queryset is evaluated. This difference in behaviour should be
   196	        taken into account when experimenting with ``for_specific_subqueries=True`` .
   197	
   198	        As with Django's native implementation, you chain multiple applications of
   199	        ``select_related()`` with ``for_specific_subqueries=True`` to progressively add
   200	        to the list of fields to be fetched. For example:
   201	
   202	        .. code-block:: python
   203	
   204	            # Fetch 'author' when retrieving specific page data
   205	            queryset = Page.objects.specific().select_related("author", for_specific_subqueries=True)
   206	
   207	            # We're rendering cards with images, so fetch the listing image too
   208	            queryset = queryset.select_related("listing_image", for_specific_subqueries=True)
   209	
   210	            # Fetch some key taxonomy data too
   211	            queryset = queryset.select_related("topic", "target_audience", for_specific_subqueries=True)
   212	
   213	        As with Django's native implementation, ``None`` can be supplied in place of
   214	        ``fields`` to negate a previous application of ``select_related()``. By default,
   215	        this will only work for cases where ``select_related()`` was called without
   216	        ``for_specific_subqueries``, or with ``for_specific_subqueries=False``. However,
   217	        you can use ``for_specific_subqueries=True`` to negate subquery-specific
   218	        applications too. For example:
   219	
   220	        .. code-block:: python
   221	
   222	            # Fetch 'author' and 'listing_image' when retrieving specific page data
   223	            queryset = Page.objects.specific().select_related(
   224	                "author",
   225	                "listing_image",
   226	                for_specific_subqueries=True
   227	            )
   228	
   229	            # I've changed my mind. Do not fetch any additional data
   230	            queryset = queryset.select_related(None, for_specific_subqueries=True)
   231	        """
   232	
   233	        if not for_specific_subqueries:
   234	            return super().select_related(*fields)
   235	        if not fields:
   236	            raise ValueError(
   237	                "'fields' must be specified when calling select_related() with for_specific_subqueries=True"
   238	            )
   239	        clone = self._chain()
   240	        if fields == (None,):
   241	            clone._specific_select_related_fields = ()
   242	        else:
   243	            clone._specific_select_related_fields = (
   244	                self._specific_select_related_fields + fields
   245	            )
   246	        return clone

   257	        might be included in the result (which can include relationships inherited from
   258	        concrete parents). Unlike when ``for_specific_subqueries`` is ``False``, no
   259	        validation is applied to ``lookups`` when the method is called. Instead, that
   260	        validation is applied for each individual subquery when the queryset is
   261	        evaluated. This difference in behaviour should be taken into account when
   262	        experimenting with ``for_specific_subqueries=True``.
   263	
   264	        As with Django's native implementation, you chain multiple applications of
   265	        ``prefetch_related()`` with ``for_specific_subqueries=True`` to progressively
   266	        add to the list of lookups to be made. For example:

   429	    def first_common_ancestor(self, include_self=False, strict=False):
   430	        """
   431	        Find the first ancestor that all pages in this queryset have in common.
   432	        For example, consider a page hierarchy like::
   433	
   434	            - Home/
   435	                - Foo Event Index/
   436	                    - Foo Event Page 1/
   437	                    - Foo Event Page 2/
   438	                - Bar Event Index/
   439	                    - Bar Event Page 1/
   440	                    - Bar Event Page 2/
   441	
   442	        The common ancestors for some queries would be:
   443	
   444	        .. code-block:: python
   445	
   446	            >>> Page.objects\\
   447	            ...     .type(EventPage)\\
   448	            ...     .first_common_ancestor()
   449	            <Page: Home>
   450	            >>> Page.objects\\
   451	            ...     .type(EventPage)\\
   452	            ...     .filter(title__contains='Foo')\\
   453	            ...     .first_common_ancestor()
   454	            <Page: Foo Event Index>
   455	
   456	        This method tries to be efficient, but if you have millions of pages
   457	        scattered across your page tree, it will be slow.
   458	
   459	        If `include_self` is True, the ancestor can be one of the pages in the
   460	        queryset:
   461	
   462	        .. code-block:: python
   463	
   464	            >>> Page.objects\\
   465	            ...     .filter(title__contains='Foo')\\
   466	            ...     .first_common_ancestor()
   467	            <Page: Foo Event Index>
   468	            >>> Page.objects\\
   469	            ...     .filter(title__exact='Bar Event Index')\\
   470	            ...     .first_common_ancestor()
   471	            <Page: Bar Event Index>
   472	
   473	        A few invalid cases exist: when the queryset is empty, when the root
   474	        Page is in the queryset and ``include_self`` is False, and when there
   475	        are multiple page trees with no common root (a case Wagtail does not
   476	        support). If ``strict`` is False (the default), then the first root
   477	        node is returned in these cases. If ``strict`` is True, then a
   478	        ``ObjectDoesNotExist`` is raised.
   479	        """
   480	        # An empty queryset has no ancestors. This is a problem
   481	        if not self.exists():
   482	            if strict:
   483	                raise self.model.DoesNotExist("Can not find ancestor of empty queryset")
   484	            return self.model.get_first_root_node()
   485	
   486	        if include_self:
   487	            # Get all the paths of the matched pages.
   488	            paths = self.order_by().values_list("path", flat=True)
   489	        else:
   490	            # Find all the distinct parent paths of all matched pages.
   491	            # The empty `.order_by()` ensures that `Page.path` is not also
   492	            # selected to order the results, which makes `.distinct()` works.
   493	            paths = (
   494	                self.order_by()
   495	                .annotate(
   496	                    parent_path=Substr(
   497	                        "path",
   498	                        1,
   499	                        Length("path") - self.model.steplen,
   500	                        output_field=CharField(max_length=255),
   501	                    )
   502	                )
   503	                .values_list("parent_path", flat=True)
   504	                .distinct()
   505	            )
   506	
   507	        # This method works on anything, not just file system paths.
   508	        common_parent_path = posixpath.commonprefix(paths)
   509	
   510	        # That may have returned a path like (0001, 0002, 000), which is
   511	        # missing some chars off the end. Fix this by trimming the path to a
   512	        # multiple of `Page.steplen`
   513	        extra_chars = len(common_parent_path) % self.model.steplen
   514	        if extra_chars != 0:
   515	            common_parent_path = common_parent_path[:-extra_chars]
   516	
   517	        if common_parent_path == "":
   518	            # This should only happen when there are multiple trees,
   519	            # a situation that Wagtail does not support;
   520	            # or when the root node itself is part of the queryset.
   521	            if strict:
   522	                raise self.model.DoesNotExist("No common ancestor found!")
   523	
   524	            # Assuming the situation is the latter, just return the root node.
   525	            # The root node is not its own ancestor, so this is technically
   526	            # incorrect. If you want very correct operation, use `strict=True`
   527	            # and receive an error.
   528	            return self.model.get_first_root_node()
   529	
   530	        # Assuming the database is in a consistent state, this page should
   531	        # *always* exist. If your database is not in a consistent state, you've
   532	        # got bigger problems.
   533	        return self.model.objects.get(path=common_parent_path)
   534	
   535	    def unpublish(self):
   536	        """
   537	        This unpublishes all live pages in the QuerySet.
   538	        """
   539	        for page in self.live():
   540	            page.unpublish()

   542	    def defer_streamfields(self):
   543	        """
   544	        Apply to a queryset to prevent fetching/decoding of StreamField values on
   545	        evaluation. Useful when working with potentially large numbers of results,
   546	        where StreamField values are unlikely to be needed. For example, when
   547	        generating a sitemap or a long list of page links.
   548	        """
   549	        clone = self._clone()
   550	        clone._defer_streamfields = True  # used by specific_iterator()
   551	        streamfield_names = self.model.get_streamfield_names()
   552	        if not streamfield_names:
   553	            return clone
   554	        return clone.defer(*streamfield_names)

    14	MATCH_HOSTNAME_PORT = 0
    15	MATCH_HOSTNAME_DEFAULT = 1
    16	MATCH_DEFAULT = 2
    17	MATCH_HOSTNAME = 3

    20	def get_site_for_hostname(hostname, port):
    21	    """Return the wagtailcore.Site object for the given hostname and port."""
    22	    Site = apps.get_model("wagtailcore.Site")
    23	
    24	    sites = list(
    25	        Site.objects.annotate(
    26	            match=Case(
    27	                # annotate the results by best choice descending
    28	                # put exact hostname+port match first
    29	                When(hostname=hostname, port=port, then=MATCH_HOSTNAME_PORT),
    30	                # then put hostname+default (better than just hostname or just default)
    31	                When(
    32	                    hostname=hostname, is_default_site=True, then=MATCH_HOSTNAME_DEFAULT
    33	                ),
    34	                # then match default with different hostname. there is only ever
    35	                # one default, so order it above (possibly multiple) hostname
    36	                # matches so we can use sites[0] below to access it
    37	                When(is_default_site=True, then=MATCH_DEFAULT),
    38	                # because of the filter below, if it's not default then its a hostname match
    39	                default=MATCH_HOSTNAME,
    40	                output_field=IntegerField(),
    41	            )
    42	        )
    43	        .filter(Q(hostname=hostname) | Q(is_default_site=True))
    44	        .order_by("match")
    45	        .select_related("root_page")
    46	    )
    47	
    48	    if sites:
    49	        # if there's a unique match or hostname (with port or default) match
    50	        if len(sites) == 1 or sites[0].match in (
    51	            MATCH_HOSTNAME_PORT,
    52	            MATCH_HOSTNAME_DEFAULT,
    53	        ):
    54	            return sites[0]
    55	
    56	        # if there is a default match with a different hostname, see if
    57	        # there are many hostname matches. if only 1 then use that instead
    58	        # otherwise we use the default
    59	        if sites[0].match == MATCH_DEFAULT:
    60	            return sites[len(sites) == 2]
    61	
    62	    raise Site.DoesNotExist()

   146	    @staticmethod
   147	    def find_for_request(request):
   148	        """
   149	        Find the site object responsible for responding to this HTTP
   150	        request object. Try:
   151

   153	        * then hostname and port
   154	        * if there is no matching hostname at all, or no matching
   155	          hostname:port combination, fall back to the unique default site,
   156	          or raise an exception

   159	        still be routed to a different hostname which is set as the default

   161	        The site will be cached via request._wagtail_site
   162	        """
   163	
   164	        if request is None:
   165	            return None
   166	
   167	        if not hasattr(request, "_wagtail_site"):
   168	            site = Site._find_for_request(request)
   169	            request._wagtail_site = site

   172	    @staticmethod

   217	    @staticmethod
   218	    def get_site_root_paths():
   219	        """
   220	        Return a list of `SiteRootPath` instances, most specific path
   221	        first - used to translate url_paths into actual URLs with hostnames.
   222	
   223	        Each root path is an instance of the `SiteRootPath` named tuple,
   224	        and have the following attributes:
   225	
   226	        - ``site_id`` - The ID of the Site record
   227	        - ``root_path`` - The internal URL path of the site's home page (for example '/home/')
   228	        - ``root_url`` - The scheme/domain name of the site (for example 'https://www.example.com/')
   229	        - ``language_code`` - The language code of the site (for example 'en')
   230	        """
   231	        result = cache.get(
   232	            SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_KEY, version=SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_VERSION
   233	        )
   234	
   235	        if result is None:
   236	            result = []
   237	
   238	            for site in Site.objects.select_related(
   239	                "root_page", "root_page__locale"
   240	            ).order_by("-root_page__url_path", "-is_default_site", "hostname"):
   241	                if getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_I18N_ENABLED", False):
   242	                    result.extend(
   243	                        [
   244	                            SiteRootPath(
   245	                                site.id,
   246	                                root_page.url_path,
   247	                                site.root_url,
   248	                                root_page.locale.language_code,
   249	                            )
   250	                            for root_page in site.root_page.get_translations(
   251	                                inclusive=True
   252	                            ).select_related("locale")
   253	                        ]
   254	                    )
   255	                else:
   256	                    result.append(
   257	                        SiteRootPath(
   258	                            site.id,
   259	                            site.root_page.url_path,
   260	                            site.root_url,
   261	                            site.root_page.locale.language_code,
   262	                        )
   263	                    )
   264	
   265	            cache.set(
   266	                SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_KEY,
   267	                result,
   268	                3600,
   269	                version=SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_VERSION,
   270	            )
   271	
   272	        else:
   273	            # Convert the cache result to a list of SiteRootPath tuples, as some
   274	            # cache backends (e.g. Redis) don't support named tuples.
   275	            result = [SiteRootPath(*result) for result in result]
   276	
   277	        return result

   280	    def clear_site_root_paths_cache():
   281	        cache.delete(SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_KEY, version=SITE_ROOT_PATHS_CACHE_VERSION)

The core contract is broader than just fields: `Page` owns tree paths/depth, URL path maintenance, content-type polymorphism, revision/workflow generic relations, aliasing, permissions, comments, and page-specific log/view-restriction models. I’m collecting the stored-reference models next because those are the places a contract move is most likely to require migrations or compatibility shims.

  1280	    def check_request_method(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
  1281	        """
  1282	        Checks the ``method`` attribute of the request against those supported
  1283	        by the page (as defined by :attr:`allowed_http_methods`) and responds
  1284	        accordingly.

  1286	        If supported, ``None`` is returned, and the request is processed
  1287	        normally. If not, a warning is logged and an ``HttpResponseNotAllowed``
  1288	        is returned, and any further request handling is terminated.

  1291	        if request.method not in allowed_methods:
  1292	            response = HttpResponseNotAllowed(allowed_methods)
  1293	            log_response(
  1294	                "Method Not Allowed (%s): %s",
  1295	                request.method,
  1296	                request.path,
  1297	                request=request,
  1298	                response=response,
  1299	            )
  1300	            return response

  1447	    def get_url(self, request=None, current_site=None):

  1451	        this is the local URL (starting with '/') if we're only running a single site
  1452	        (i.e. we know that whatever the current page is being served from, this link will be on the
  1453	        same domain), and the full URL (with domain) if not.

  1457	        be used to cache site-level URL information (thereby avoiding repeated database / cache
  1458	        lookups) and, via the ``Site.find_for_request()`` function, determine whether a relative
  1459	        or full URL is most appropriate.

  1462	        # a relative URL based on ``Site.find_for_request()``. Nonetheless, support it here to avoid

  1469	        if url_parts is None or url_parts[1] is None and url_parts[2] is None:
  1470	            # page is not routable
  1471	            return

  1477	        num_sites = len(

  1624	        instance.

  1632	        """

  1634	        page instance.
  1635	        """

  1637	
  1638	        if cls.max_count is not None:
  1639	            can_create = can_create and cls.objects.count() < cls.max_count
  1640	
  1641	        if cls.max_count_per_parent is not None:
  1642	            can_create = (
  1643	                can_create
  1644	                and parent.get_children().type(cls).count() < cls.max_count_per_parent
  1645	            )

  1649	    def can_move_to(self, parent):

  1652	        page instance.

  1661	        if not self.can_exist_under(parent):

  1664	        # If the page has a max_count_per_parent, check for a page of this type under the destination
  1665	        if self.max_count_per_parent is not None:
  1666	            return (
  1667	                parent.get_children().type(self.specific_class).not_page(self).count()
  1668	                < self.max_count_per_parent
  1669	            )

  1671	        return True

  1925	    def get_view_restrictions(self):

  1936	
  1937	        def add_page_to_check_list(page):

  1939	            if page.alias_of:
  1940	                add_page_to_check_list(page.alias_of)
  1941	            else:
  1942	                page_ids_to_check.add(page.id)
  1943	
  1944	        # Check current page for view restrictions
  1945	        add_page_to_check_list(self)
  1946	
  1947	        # Check each ancestor for view restrictions as well
  1948	        for page in self.get_ancestors().only("alias_of"):

  1967	            "wagtailcore/password_required.html",
  1968	        )

  1973	        if not hasattr(request, "preview_mode"):

  1976	        context = self.get_context(request)

  1981	    def with_content_json(self, content):
  1982	        """
  1983	        Returns a new version of the page with field values updated to reflect changes
  1984	        in the provided ``content`` (which usually comes from a previously-saved
  1985	        page revision).
  1986	
  1987	        Certain field values are preserved in order to prevent errors if the returned
  1988	        page is saved, such as ``id``, ``content_type`` and some tree-related values.
  1989	        The following field values are also preserved, as they are considered to be
  1990	        meaningful to the page as a whole, rather than to a specific revision:
  1991	
  1992	        * ``draft_title``
  1993	        * ``live``
  1994	        * ``has_unpublished_changes``
  1995	        * ``owner``
  1996	        * ``locked``
  1997	        * ``locked_by``
  1998	        * ``locked_at``
  1999	        * ``latest_revision``
  2000	        * ``latest_revision_created_at``
  2001	        * ``first_published_at``
  2002	        * ``alias_of``
  2003	        * ``wagtail_admin_comments`` (COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME)
  2004	        """
  2005	
  2006	        # Old revisions (pre Wagtail 2.15) may have saved comment data under the name 'comments'
  2007	        # rather than the current relation name as set by COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME;
  2008	        # if a 'comments' field exists and looks like our comments model, alter the data to use
  2009	        # COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME before restoring
  2010	        if (
  2011	            COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME not in content
  2012	            and "comments" in content
  2013	            and isinstance(content["comments"], list)
  2014	            and len(content["comments"])
  2015	            and isinstance(content["comments"][0], dict)
  2016	            and "contentpath" in content["comments"][0]
  2017	        ):
  2018	            content[COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME] = content["comments"]
  2019	            del content["comments"]
  2020	
  2021	        obj = self.specific_class.from_serializable_data(content)
  2022	
  2023	        # These should definitely never change between revisions
  2024	        obj.id = self.id
  2025	        obj.pk = self.pk
  2026	        obj.content_type_id = self.content_type_id
  2027	
  2028	        # Override possibly-outdated tree parameter fields
  2029	        obj.path = self.path
  2030	        obj.depth = self.depth
  2031	        obj.numchild = self.numchild
  2032	
  2033	        # Update url_path to reflect potential slug changes, but maintaining the page's
  2034	        # existing tree position
  2035	        obj.set_url_path(self.get_parent())
  2036	
  2037	        # Ensure other values that are meaningful for the page as a whole (rather than
  2038	        # to a specific revision) are preserved
  2039	        obj.draft_title = self.draft_title
  2040	        obj.live = self.live
  2041	        obj.has_unpublished_changes = self.has_unpublished_changes
  2042	        obj.owner_id = self.owner_id
  2043	        obj.locked = self.locked
  2044	        obj.locked_by_id = self.locked_by_id
  2045	        obj.locked_at = self.locked_at
  2046	        obj.latest_revision_id = self.latest_revision_id
  2047	        obj.latest_revision_created_at = self.latest_revision_created_at
  2048	
  2049	        if obj.first_published_at is None:
  2050	            obj.first_published_at = self.first_published_at
  2051	
  2052	        obj.translation_key = self.translation_key
  2053	        obj.locale_id = self.locale_id
  2054	        obj.alias_of_id = self.alias_of_id
  2055	        revision_comment_positions = dict(
  2056	            getattr(obj, COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME).values_list("id", "position")
  2057	        )
  2058	        page_comments = (
  2059	            getattr(self, COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME)
  2060	            .filter(resolved_at__isnull=True)
  2061	            .defer("position")
  2062	        )
  2063	        for comment in page_comments:
  2064	            # attempt to retrieve the comment position from the revision's stored version
  2065	            # of the comment
  2066	            try:
  2067	                comment.position = revision_comment_positions[comment.id]
  2068	            except KeyError:
  2069	                pass
  2070	        setattr(obj, COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME, page_comments)
  2071	
  2072	        return obj

  2075	    def has_workflow(self):

  2088	    def get_workflow(self):
  2089	        """
  2090	        Returns the active workflow assigned to the page or its nearest ancestor.
  2091	        """
  2092	        if not getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_WORKFLOW_ENABLED", True):
  2093	            return None
  2094	
  2095	        if hasattr(self, "workflowpage") and self.workflowpage.workflow.active:
  2096	            return self.workflowpage.workflow
  2097	        else:
  2098	            try:
  2099	                workflow = (
  2100	                    self.get_ancestors()
  2101	                    .filter(workflowpage__isnull=False)
  2102	                    .filter(workflowpage__workflow__active=True)
  2103	                    .order_by("-depth")
  2104	                    .first()
  2105	                    .workflowpage.workflow
  2106	                )
  2107	            except AttributeError:
  2108	                workflow = None
  2109	            return workflow

  2133	class GroupPagePermissionManager(models.Manager):
  2134	    def create(self, **kwargs):
  2135	        # Simplify creation of GroupPagePermission objects by allowing one
  2136	        # of permission or permission_type to be passed in.
  2137	        permission = kwargs.get("permission")
  2138	        permission_type = kwargs.pop("permission_type", None)
  2139	        if not permission and permission_type:
  2140	            kwargs["permission"] = Permission.objects.get(
  2141	                content_type=get_default_page_content_type(),
  2142	                codename=f"{permission_type}_page",
  2143	            )
  2144	        return super().create(**kwargs)

    30	class RevisionQuerySet(models.QuerySet):
    31	    def page_revisions_q(self):
    32	        return Q(base_content_type=get_default_page_content_type())
    33	
    34	    def page_revisions(self):
    35	        return self.filter(self.page_revisions_q())
    36	
    37	    def not_page_revisions(self):
    38	        return self.exclude(self.page_revisions_q())
    39	
    40	    def for_instance(self, instance):
    41	        try:
    42	            # Use RevisionMixin.get_base_content_type() if available
    43	            return self.filter(
    44	                base_content_type=instance.get_base_content_type(),
    45	                object_id=str(instance.pk),
    46	            )
    47	        except AttributeError:
    48	            # Fallback to ContentType for the model
    49	            return self.filter(
    50	                content_type=ContentType.objects.get_for_model(
    51	                    instance, for_concrete_model=False
    52	                ),
    53	                object_id=str(instance.pk),
    54	            )

    57	class RevisionsManager(models.Manager.from_queryset(RevisionQuerySet)):
    58	    def previous_revision_id_subquery(self, revision_fk_name="revision"):
    59	        """
    60	        Returns a Subquery that can be used to annotate a queryset with the ID
    61	        of the previous revision, based on the revision_fk_name field. Useful
    62	        to avoid N+1 queries when generating comparison links between revisions.
    63	
    64	        The logic is similar to ``Revision.get_previous().pk``.
    65	        """
    66	        fk = revision_fk_name
    67	        return Subquery(
    68	            Revision.objects.filter(
    69	                base_content_type_id=OuterRef(f"{fk}__base_content_type_id"),
    70	                object_id=OuterRef(f"{fk}__object_id"),
    71	            )
    72	            .filter(
    73	                Q(
    74	                    created_at=OuterRef(f"{fk}__created_at"),
    75	                    pk__lt=OuterRef(f"{fk}__pk"),
    76	                )
    77	                | Q(created_at__lt=OuterRef(f"{fk}__created_at"))
    78	            )
    79	            .order_by("-created_at", "-pk")
    80	            .values_list("pk", flat=True)[:1]
    81	        )

   130	    def save(self, user=None, *args, **kwargs):
   131	        # Set default value for created_at to now
   132	        # We cannot use auto_now_add as that will override
   133	        # any value that is set before saving
   134	        if self.created_at is None:
   135	            self.created_at = timezone.now()
   136	
   137	        # Set default value for base_content_type to the content_type.
   138	        # Page revisions should set this to the default Page model's content type,
   139	        # but the distinction may not be necessary for models that do not use inheritance.
   140	        if self.base_content_type_id is None:
   141	            self.base_content_type_id = self.content_type_id
   142	
   143	        super().save(*args, **kwargs)
   144	
   145	        if (
   146	            self.approved_go_live_at is None
   147	            and "update_fields" in kwargs
   148	            and "approved_go_live_at" in kwargs["update_fields"]
   149	        ):
   150	            # Log scheduled revision publish cancellation
   151	            object = self.as_object()
   152	            # go_live_at = kwargs['update_fields'][]
   153	            log(
   154	                instance=object,
   155	                action="wagtail.schedule.cancel",
   156	                data={
   157	                    "revision": {
   158	                        "id": self.id,
   159	                        "created": ensure_utc(self.created_at),
   160	                        "go_live_at": ensure_utc(object.go_live_at)
   161	                        if object.go_live_at
   162	                        else None,
   163	                        "has_live_version": object.live,
   164	                    }
   165	                },
   166	                user=user,

   173	    def is_latest_revision(self):
   174	        if self.id is None:
   175	            # special case: a revision without an ID is presumed to be newly-created and is thus
   176	            # newer than any revision that might exist in the database
   177	            return True
   178	
   179	        latest_revision_id = (
   180	            Revision.objects.filter(
   181	                base_content_type_id=self.base_content_type_id,
   182	                object_id=self.object_id,
   183	            )
   184	            .order_by("-created_at", "-id")
   185	            .values_list("id", flat=True)
   186	            .first()
   187	        )
   188	        return latest_revision_id == self.id

   190	    def delete(self):
   191	        # Update revision_created fields for comments that reference the current revision, if applicable.
   192	
   193	        try:
   194	            next_revision = self.get_next()
   195	        except Revision.DoesNotExist:
   196	            next_revision = None
   197	
   198	        if next_revision:
   199	            # move comments created on this revision to the next revision, as they may well still apply if they're unresolved
   200	            self.created_comments.all().update(revision_created=next_revision)
   201	
   202	        return super().delete()

   251	class RevisionMixin(models.Model):
   252	    """A mixin that allows a model to have revisions."""
   253	
   254	    latest_revision = models.ForeignKey(
   255	        "wagtailcore.Revision",
   256	        related_name="+",
   257	        verbose_name=_("latest revision"),
   258	        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
   259	        null=True,
   260	        blank=True,
   261	        editable=False,
   262	    )
   263	
   264	    _revisions = GenericRelation(
   265	        "wagtailcore.Revision",
   266	        content_type_field="content_type",
   267	        object_id_field="object_id",
   268	        for_concrete_model=False,
   269	    )
   270	    """
   271	    A default ``GenericRelation`` for the purpose of automatically deleting
   272	    revisions when the object is deleted. This is not used to query the object's
   273	    revisions. Instead, the :meth:`revisions` property is used for that purpose.
   274	    As such, this default relation is considered private.
   275	
   276	    This ``GenericRelation`` does not have a
   277	    :attr:`~django.contrib.contenttypes.fields.GenericRelation.related_query_name`,
   278	    so it cannot be used for reverse-related queries from ``Revision`` back to
   279	    this model. If the feature is desired, subclasses can define their own
   280	    ``GenericRelation`` to ``Revision`` with a custom ``related_query_name``.
   281	    """
   282	
   283	    # An array of additional field names that will not be included when the object is copied.
   284	    default_exclude_fields_in_copy = [
   285	        "latest_revision",
   286	    ]
   287	
   288	    @property
   289	    def revisions(self):
   290	        """
   291	        Returns revisions that belong to the object. For non-page models, this
   292	        is done by querying the :class:`~wagtail.models.Revision` model directly
   293	        rather than using a
   294	        :class:`~django.contrib.contenttypes.fields.GenericRelation`, to avoid
   295	        `a known limitation <https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/31269>`_ in
   296	        Django for models with multi-table inheritance where the relation's
   297	        content type may not match the instance's type.
   298	        """
   299	        return Revision.objects.for_instance(self)
   300	
   301	    @classmethod
   302	    def get_base_content_type(cls):
   303	        parents = cls._meta.get_parent_list()
   304	        # Get the last non-abstract parent in the MRO as the base_content_type.
   305	        # Note: for_concrete_model=False means that the model can be a proxy model.
   306	        if parents:
   307	            return ContentType.objects.get_for_model(
   308	                parents[-1], for_concrete_model=False
   309	            )
   310	        # This model doesn't inherit from a non-abstract model,
   311	        # use it as the base_content_type.
   312	        return ContentType.objects.get_for_model(cls, for_concrete_model=False)
   313	
   314	    @classmethod
   315	    def get_content_type(cls):
   316	        return ContentType.objects.get_for_model(cls, for_concrete_model=False)
   317	
   318	    def get_latest_revision(self):
   319	        return self.latest_revision
   320	
   321	    def get_latest_revision_as_object(self):
   322	        """
   323	        Returns the latest revision of the object as an instance of the model.
   324	        If no latest revision exists, returns the object itself.
   325	        """
   326	        latest_revision = self.get_latest_revision()
   327	        if latest_revision:
   328	            return latest_revision.as_object()
   329	        return self

    13	class DraftStateMixin(models.Model):
    14	    live = models.BooleanField(verbose_name=_("live"), default=True, editable=False)
    15	    has_unpublished_changes = models.BooleanField(
    16	        verbose_name=_("has unpublished changes"), default=False, editable=False
    17	    )
    18	
    19	    first_published_at = models.DateTimeField(
    20	        verbose_name=_("first published at"), blank=True, null=True, db_index=True
    21	    )
    22	    last_published_at = models.DateTimeField(
    23	        verbose_name=_("last published at"), null=True, editable=False
    24	    )
    25	    live_revision = models.ForeignKey(
    26	        "wagtailcore.Revision",
    27	        related_name="+",
    28	        verbose_name=_("live revision"),
    29	        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
    30	        null=True,
    31	        blank=True,
    32	        editable=False,
    33	    )
    34	
    35	    go_live_at = models.DateTimeField(
    36	        verbose_name=_("go live date/time"), blank=True, null=True
    37	    )
    38	    expire_at = models.DateTimeField(
    39	        verbose_name=_("expiry date/time"), blank=True, null=True
    40	    )
    41	    expired = models.BooleanField(
    42	        verbose_name=_("expired"), default=False, editable=False
    43	    )

    45	    class Meta:
    46	        abstract = True
    47	
    48	    @classmethod
    49	    def check(cls, **kwargs):
    50	        return [
    51	            *super().check(**kwargs),
    52	            *cls._check_revision_mixin(),
    53	        ]
    54	
    55	    @classmethod
    56	    def _check_revision_mixin(cls):
    57	        mro = cls.mro()
    58	        error = checks.Error(
    59	            "DraftStateMixin requires RevisionMixin to be applied after DraftStateMixin.",
    60	            hint="Add RevisionMixin to the model's base classes after DraftStateMixin.",
    61	            obj=cls,
    62	            id="wagtailcore.E004",
    63	        )
    64	
    65	        try:
    66	            if mro.index(RevisionMixin) < mro.index(DraftStateMixin):
    67	                return [error]
    68	        except ValueError:
    69	            return [error]
    70	
    71	        return []

    73	    @property
    74	    def approved_schedule(self):
    75	        return self.scheduled_revision is not None
    76	
    77	    @property
    78	    def status_string(self):
    79	        if not self.live:
    80	            if self.expired:
    81	                return _("expired")
    82	            elif self.approved_schedule:
    83	                return _("scheduled")
    84	            else:
    85	                return _("draft")
    86	        else:
    87	            if self.approved_schedule:
    88	                return _("live + scheduled")
    89	            elif self.has_unpublished_changes:
    90	                return _("live + draft")
    91	            else:
    92	                return _("live")

    94	    def publish(
    95	        self,
    96	        revision,
    97	        user=None,
    98	        changed=True,
    99	        log_action=True,
   100	        previous_revision=None,
   101	        skip_permission_checks=False,
   102	    ):
   103	        """
   104	        Publish a revision of the object by applying the changes in the revision to the live object.
   105	
   106	        :param revision: Revision to publish.
   107	        :type revision: Revision
   108	        :param user: The publishing user.
   109	        :param changed: Indicated whether content has changed.
   110	        :param log_action: Flag for the logging action, pass ``False`` to skip logging.
   111	        :param previous_revision: Indicates a revision reversal. Should be set to the previous revision instance.
   112	        :type previous_revision: Revision
   113	        """
   114	        return PublishRevisionAction(
   115	            revision,
   116	            user=user,
   117	            changed=changed,
   118	            log_action=log_action,
   119	            previous_revision=previous_revision,
   120	        ).execute(skip_permission_checks=skip_permission_checks)
   121	
   122	    def unpublish(self, set_expired=False, commit=True, user=None, log_action=True):
   123	        """
   124	        Unpublish the live object.
   125	
   126	        :param set_expired: Mark the object as expired.
   127	        :param commit: Commit the changes to the database.
   128	        :param user: The unpublishing user.
   129	        :param log_action: Flag for the logging action, pass ``False`` to skip logging.
   130	        """
   131	        return UnpublishAction(
   132	            self,
   133	            set_expired=set_expired,
   134	            commit=commit,
   135	            user=user,
   136	            log_action=log_action,
   137	        ).execute()
   138	
   139	    def with_content_json(self, content):
   140	        """
   141	        Similar to :meth:`RevisionMixin.with_content_json`,
   142	        but with the following fields also preserved:
   143	
   144	        * ``live``
   145	        * ``has_unpublished_changes``
   146	        * ``first_published_at``
   147	        """
   148	        obj = super().with_content_json(content)
   149	
   150	        # Ensure other values that are meaningful for the object as a whole (rather than
   151	        # to a specific revision) are preserved
   152	        obj.live = self.live
   153	        obj.has_unpublished_changes = self.has_unpublished_changes
   154	        obj.first_published_at = self.first_published_at
   155	
   156	        return obj
   157	
   158	    def get_latest_revision_as_object(self):
   159	        if not self.has_unpublished_changes:
   160	            # Use the live database copy in preference to the revision record, as:
   161	            # 1) this will pick up any changes that have been made directly to the model,
   162	            #    such as automated data imports;
   163	            # 2) it ensures that inline child objects pick up real database IDs even if
   164	            #    those are absent from the revision data. (If this wasn't the case, the child
   165	            #    objects would be recreated with new IDs on next publish - see #1853)
   166	            return self
   167	
   168	        latest_revision = self.get_latest_revision()
   169	
   170	        if latest_revision:
   171	            return latest_revision.as_object()
   172	        else:
   173	            return self
   174	
   175	    @cached_property
   176	    def scheduled_revision(self):
   177	        return self.revisions.filter(approved_go_live_at__isnull=False).first()
   178	
   179	    def get_scheduled_revision_as_object(self):
   180	        scheduled_revision = self.scheduled_revision
   181	        return scheduled_revision and scheduled_revision.as_object()
   182	
   183	    def _update_from_revision(self, revision, changed=True):
   184	        update_fields = ["latest_revision"]
   185	        self.latest_revision = revision
   186	
   187	        if changed:
   188	            self.has_unpublished_changes = True
   189	            update_fields.append("has_unpublished_changes")
   190	
   191	        self.save(update_fields=update_fields)
   192	
   193	    def get_lock(self):
   194	        # Scheduled publishing lock should take precedence over other locks
   195	        if self.approved_schedule:
   196	            return ScheduledForPublishLock(self)

    84	                object_id=str(instance.pk),
    85	            )
    86	        except AttributeError:
    87	            # Fallback to ContentType for the model
    88	            return self.filter(
    89	                content_type=ContentType.objects.get_for_model(
    90	                    instance, for_concrete_model=False
    91	                ),
    92	                object_id=str(instance.pk),
    93	            )

    99	class WorkflowState(models.Model):
   100	    """Tracks the status of a started Workflow on an object."""
   101	
   102	    STATUS_IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
   103	    STATUS_APPROVED = "approved"
   104	    STATUS_NEEDS_CHANGES = "needs_changes"
   105	    STATUS_CANCELLED = "cancelled"
   106	    STATUS_CHOICES = (
   107	        (STATUS_IN_PROGRESS, _("In progress")),
   108	        (STATUS_APPROVED, _("Approved")),
   109	        (STATUS_NEEDS_CHANGES, _("Needs changes")),
   110	        (STATUS_CANCELLED, _("Cancelled")),
   111	    )
   112	
   113	    content_type = models.ForeignKey(
   114	        ContentType, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name="+"
   115	    )
   116	    base_content_type = models.ForeignKey(
   117	        ContentType, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name="+"
   118	    )
   119	    object_id = models.CharField(max_length=255, verbose_name=_("object id"))
   120	
   121	    content_object = GenericForeignKey(
   122	        "base_content_type", "object_id", for_concrete_model=False
   123	    )
   124	    content_object.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True
   125	
   126	    workflow = models.ForeignKey(
   127	        "Workflow",
   128	        on_delete=models.CASCADE,
   129	        verbose_name=_("workflow"),
   130	        related_name="workflow_states",
   131	    )
   132	    status = models.fields.CharField(
   133	        choices=STATUS_CHOICES,
   134	        verbose_name=_("status"),
   135	        max_length=50,
   136	        default=STATUS_IN_PROGRESS,
   137	    )
   138	    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True, verbose_name=_("created at"))
   139	    requested_by = models.ForeignKey(
   140	        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
   141	        verbose_name=_("requested by"),
   142	        null=True,
   143	        blank=True,
   144	        editable=True,
   145	        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
   146	        related_name="requested_workflows",
   147	    )
   148	    current_task_state = models.OneToOneField(
   149	        "TaskState",
   150	        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
   151	        null=True,
   152	        blank=True,
   153	        verbose_name=_("current task state"),
   154	    )
   155	
   156	    # allows a custom function to be called on finishing the Workflow successfully.
   157	    on_finish = import_string(
   158	        getattr(
   159	            settings,
   160	            "WAGTAIL_FINISH_WORKFLOW_ACTION",
   161	            "wagtail.workflows.publish_workflow_state",
   162	        )
   163	    )
   164	
   165	    objects = WorkflowStateManager()
   166	
   167	    def clean(self):
   168	        super().clean()
   169	
   170	        if self.status in (self.STATUS_IN_PROGRESS, self.STATUS_NEEDS_CHANGES):
   171	            # The unique constraint is conditional, and so not supported on the MySQL backend - so an additional check is done here
   172	            if (
   173	                WorkflowState.objects.active()
   174	                .filter(
   175	                    base_content_type_id=self.base_content_type_id,
   176	                    object_id=self.object_id,
   177	                )
   178	                .exclude(pk=self.pk)
   179	                .exists()
   180	            ):
   181	                raise ValidationError(
   182	                    _(
   183	                        "There may only be one in progress or needs changes workflow state per page/snippet."
   184	                    )
   185	                )
   186	
   187	    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
   188	        self.full_clean()
   189	        return super().save(*args, **kwargs)
   190	
   191	    def __str__(self):
   192	        return _(
   193	            "Workflow '%(workflow_name)s' on %(model_name)s '%(title)s': %(status)s"
   194	        ) % {
   195	            "workflow_name": self.workflow,
   196	            "model_name": self.content_object._meta.verbose_name,
   197	            "title": self.content_object,
   198	            "status": self.status,
   199	        }
   200	
   201	    def resume(self, user=None):
   202	        """Put a STATUS_NEEDS_CHANGES workflow state back into STATUS_IN_PROGRESS, and restart the current task"""
   203	        from wagtail.models import Page
   204	
   205	        if self.status != self.STATUS_NEEDS_CHANGES:
   206	            raise PermissionDenied
   207	        revision = self.current_task_state.revision
   208	        current_task_state = self.current_task_state
   209	        self.current_task_state = None
   210	        self.status = self.STATUS_IN_PROGRESS
   211	        self.save()
   212	
   213	        instance = self.content_object
   214	        if isinstance(instance, Page):
   215	            instance = self.content_object.specific
   216	
   217	        log(
   218	            instance=instance,
   219	            action="wagtail.workflow.resume",
   220	            data={
   221	                "workflow": {
   222	                    "id": self.workflow_id,
   223	                    "title": self.workflow.name,
   224	                    "status": self.status,
   225	                    "task_state_id": current_task_state.id,
   226	                    "task": {
   227	                        "id": current_task_state.task.id,
   228	                        "title": current_task_state.task.name,
   229	                    },
   230	                }
   231	            },
   232	            revision=revision,
   233	            user=user,
   234	        )
   235	        return self.update(user=user, next_task=current_task_state.task)
   236	
   237	    def user_can_cancel(self, user):
   238	        if (
   239	            isinstance(self.content_object, LockableMixin)
   240	            and self.content_object.locked
   241	            and self.content_object.locked_by != user
   242	        ):
   243	            return False
   244	        return (
   245	            user == self.requested_by
   246	            or user == getattr(self.content_object, "owner", None)
   247	            or (
   248	                self.current_task_state
   249	                and self.current_task_state.status
   250	                == self.current_task_state.STATUS_IN_PROGRESS
   251	                and "approve"
   252	                in [
   253	                    action[0]
   254	                    for action in self.current_task_state.task.specific.get_actions(
   255	                        self.content_object, user
   256	                    )
   257	                ]
   258	            )
   259	        )

    22	class ReferenceGroups:
    23	    """
    24	    Groups records in a ReferenceIndex queryset by their source object.
    25	
    26	    Args:
    27	        qs: (QuerySet[ReferenceIndex] | list[ReferenceIndex]) A QuerySet or list of ReferenceIndex instances
    28	
    29	    Yields:
    30	        A tuple (source_object, references) for each source object that appears
    31	        in the queryset. source_object is the model instance of the source object
    32	        and references is a list of references that occur in the QuerySet from
    33	        that source object.
    34	    """
    35	
    36	    def __init__(self, qs):
    37	        if isinstance(qs, models.QuerySet):
    38	            self.qs = qs.order_by("base_content_type", "object_id")
    39	        else:
    40	            self.qs = sorted(qs, key=lambda x: (x.base_content_type_id, x.object_id))
    41	
    42	    def __iter__(self):
    43	        reference_fk = None
    44	        references = []
    45	        for reference in self.qs:
    46	            if reference_fk != (reference.base_content_type_id, reference.object_id):
    47	                if reference_fk is not None:
    48	                    content_type = ContentType.objects.get_for_id(reference_fk[0])
    49	                    object = content_type.get_object_for_this_type(pk=reference_fk[1])
    50	                    yield object, references
    51	                    references = []
    52	
    53	                reference_fk = (reference.base_content_type_id, reference.object_id)
    54	
    55	            references.append(reference)
    56	
    57	        if references:
    58	            content_type = ContentType.objects.get_for_id(reference_fk[0])
    59	            object = content_type.get_object_for_this_type(pk=reference_fk[1])
    60	            yield object, references
    61	
    62	    def __len__(self):
    63	        return self._count
    64	
    65	    @cached_property
    66	    def _count(self):
    67	        if isinstance(self.qs, models.QuerySet) and not self.qs._result_cache:
    68	            return self.qs.values("base_content_type", "object_id").distinct().count()
    69	        return len(
    70	            {
    71	                (reference.base_content_type_id, reference.object_id)
    72	                for reference in self.qs
    73	            }
    74	        )
    75	
    76	    @cached_property
    77	    def is_protected(self):
    78	        return any(reference.on_delete == models.PROTECT for reference in self.qs)
    79	
    80	    def count(self):
    81	        """
    82	        Returns the number of rows that will be returned by iterating this
    83	        ReferenceGroups.
    84	
    85	        Just calls len(self) internally, this method only exists to allow
    86	        instances of this class to be used in a Paginator.
    87	        """
    88	        return len(self)
    89	
    90	    def __getitem__(self, key):
    91	        return list(self)[key]
    92	
    93	
    94	class ReferenceIndexQuerySet(models.QuerySet):
    95	    def group_by_source_object(self):
    96	        """
    97	        Returns a ReferenceGroups object for this queryset that will yield
    98	        references grouped by their source instance.
    99	        """
   100	        return ReferenceGroups(self)
   101	
   102	    def group_by_source_object_in_bulk(self):
   103	        """
   104	        Returns a dict that maps (to_content_type_id, to_object_id) to a
   105	        ReferenceGroups object for this queryset that will yield references
   106	        grouped by their source instance for each referenced instance.
   107	        """
   108	        qs = self.order_by(
   109	            "to_content_type", "to_object_id", "base_content_type", "object_id"
   110	        )
   111	        return {
   112	            key: ReferenceGroups(list(references))
   113	            for key, references in groupby(
   114	                qs, key=lambda x: (x.to_content_type_id, x.to_object_id)
   115	            )
   116	        }
