   119	class ReferenceIndex(models.Model):
   120	    """
   121	    Records references between objects for quick retrieval of object usage.
   122	
   123	    References are extracted from Foreign Keys, Chooser Blocks in StreamFields, and links in Rich Text Fields.
   124	    This index allows us to efficiently find all of the references to a particular object from all of these sources.
   125	    """
   126	
   127	    # The object where the reference was extracted from
   128	
   129	    # content_type represents the content type of the model that contains
   130	    # the field where the reference came from. If the model sub-classes another
   131	    # concrete model (such as Page), that concrete model will be set in
   132	    # base_content_type, otherwise it would be the same as content_type
   133	    content_type = models.ForeignKey(
   134	        ContentType, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name="+"
   135	    )
   136	    base_content_type = models.ForeignKey(
   137	        ContentType, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name="+"
   138	    )
   139	    object_id = models.CharField(
   140	        max_length=255,
   141	        verbose_name=_("object id"),
   142	    )
   143	
   144	    # The object that has been referenced
   145	    # to_content_type is always the base content type of the referenced object
   146	    to_content_type = models.ForeignKey(
   147	        ContentType, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name="+"
   148	    )
   149	    to_object_id = models.CharField(
   150	        max_length=255,
   151	        verbose_name=_("object id"),
   152	    )
   153	
   154	    # The model_path is the path to the field on content_type where the reference was extracted from.
   155	    # the content_path is the path to a specific block on the instance where the reference is extracted from.
   156	
   157	    # These are dotted path, always starting with a field or child relation name. If
   158	    # the reference was extracted from an inline panel or streamfield, other components
   159	    # of the path can be used to locate where the reference was extracted.
   160	    #
   161	    # For example, say we have a StreamField called 'body' which has a struct block type
   162	    # called 'my_struct_block' that has a field called 'my_field'. If we extracted a
   163	    # reference from that field, the model_path would be set to the following:
   164	    #
   165	    # 'body.my_struct_block.my_field'
   166	    #
   167	    # The content path would follow the same format, but anything repeatable would be replaced by an ID.
   168	    # For example:
   169	    #
   170	    # 'body.bdc70d8b-e7a2-4c2a-bf43-2a3e3fcbbe86.my_field'
   171	    #
   172	    # We can use the model_path with the 'content_type' to find the original definition of
   173	    # the field block and display information to the user about where the reference was
   174	    # extracted from.
   175	    #
   176	    # We can use the content_path to link the user directly to the block/field that contains
   177	    # the reference.
   178	    model_path = models.TextField()
   179	    content_path = models.TextField()
   180	
   181	    # We need a separate hash field for content_path in order to use it in a unique key because
   182	    # MySQL has a limit to the size of fields that are included in unique keys
   183	    content_path_hash = models.UUIDField()
   184	
   185	    objects = ReferenceIndexQuerySet.as_manager()
   186	
   187	    wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True
   188	
   189	    # The set of models that should have signals attached to watch for outbound references.
   190	    # This includes those registered with `register_model`, as well as their child models
   191	    # linked by a ParentalKey.
   192	    tracked_models = set()
   193	
   194	    # The set of models that can appear as the 'from' object in the reference index.
   195	    # This only includes those registered with `register_model`, and NOT child models linked
   196	    # by ParentalKey (object references on those are recorded under the parent).
   197	    indexed_models = set()
   198	
   199	    class Meta:
   200	        unique_together = [
   201	            (
   202	                "base_content_type",
   203	                "object_id",
   204	                "to_content_type",
   205	                "to_object_id",
   206	                "content_path_hash",
   207	            )
   208	        ]
   209	        indexes = [
   210	            models.Index(
   211	                name="referenceindex_source_object",
   212	                fields=["base_content_type", "object_id"],
   213	            ),
   214	            models.Index(
   215	                name="referenceindex_target_object",
   216	                fields=["to_content_type", "to_object_id"],
   217	            ),
   218	        ]
   219	
   220	    @classmethod
   221	    def _get_base_content_type(cls, model_or_object):
   222	        """
   223	        Returns the ContentType record that represents the base model of the
   224	        given model or object.
   225	
   226	        For a model that uses multi-table-inheritance, this returns the model
   227	        that contains the primary key. For example, for any page object, this
   228	        will return the content type of the Page model.
   229	        """
   230	        parents = model_or_object._meta.get_parent_list()
   231	        if parents:
   232	            return ContentType.objects.get_for_model(
   233	                parents[-1], for_concrete_model=False
   234	            )
   235	        else:
   236	            return ContentType.objects.get_for_model(
   237	                model_or_object, for_concrete_model=False
   238	            )
   239	
   240	    @classmethod
   241	    def model_is_indexable(cls, model, allow_child_models=False):
   242	        """
   243	        Returns True if the given model may have outbound references that we would be interested in recording in the index.
   244	
   245	
   246	        Args:
   247	            model (type): a Django model class
   248	            allow_child_models (boolean): Child models are not indexable on their own. If you are looking at
   249	                                          a child model from the perspective of indexing it through its parent,
   250	                                          set this to True to disable checking for this. Default False.
   251	        """
   252	        if getattr(model, "wagtail_reference_index_ignore", False):
   253	            return False
   254	
   255	        # Don't check any models that have a parental key, references from these will be collected from the parent
   256	        if not allow_child_models and any(
   257	            isinstance(field, ParentalKey) for field in model._meta.get_fields()
   258	        ):
   259	            return False
   260	
   261	        for field in model._meta.get_fields():
   262	            if field.is_relation and field.many_to_one:
   263	                if getattr(field, "wagtail_reference_index_ignore", False):
   264	                    continue
   265	
   266	                if getattr(
   267	                    field.related_model, "wagtail_reference_index_ignore", False
   268	                ):
   269	                    continue
   270	
   271	                if isinstance(field, (ParentalKey, GenericRel)):
   272	                    continue
   273	
   274	                return True
   275	
   276	            if hasattr(field, "extract_references"):
   277	                return True
   278	
   279	        if issubclass(model, ClusterableModel):
   280	            for child_relation in get_all_child_relations(model):
   281	                if cls.model_is_indexable(
   282	                    child_relation.related_model,
   283	                    allow_child_models=True,
   284	                ):
   285	                    return True
   286	
   287	        return False
   288	
   289	    @classmethod
   290	    def register_model(cls, model):
   291	        """
   292	        Registers the model for indexing.
   293	        """
   294	        if model in cls.indexed_models:
   295	            return
   296	
   297	        if cls.model_is_indexable(model):
   298	            cls.indexed_models.add(model)
   299	            cls._register_as_tracked_model(model)
   300	
   301	    @classmethod
   302	    def _register_as_tracked_model(cls, model):
   303	        """
   304	        Add the model and all of its ParentalKey-linked children to the set of
   305	        models to be tracked by signal handlers.
   306	        """
   307	        if model in cls.tracked_models:
   308	            return
   309	
   310	        from wagtail.signal_handlers import (
   311	            connect_reference_index_signal_handlers_for_model,
   312	        )
   313	
   314	        cls.tracked_models.add(model)
   315	        connect_reference_index_signal_handlers_for_model(model)
   316	
   317	        for child_relation in get_all_child_relations(model):
   318	            if cls.model_is_indexable(
   319	                child_relation.related_model,
   320	                allow_child_models=True,
   321	            ):
   322	                cls._register_as_tracked_model(child_relation.related_model)
   323	
   324	    @classmethod
   325	    def is_indexed(cls, model):
   326	        return model in cls.indexed_models
   327	
   328	    @classmethod
   329	    def _extract_references_from_object(cls, object):
   330	        """
   331	        Generator that scans the given object and yields any references it finds.
   332	
   333	        Args:
   334	            object (Model): an instance of a Django model to scan for references
   335	
   336	        Yields:
   337	            A tuple (content_type_id, object_id, model_path, content_path) for each
   338	            reference found.
   339	
   340	            content_type_id (int): The ID of the ContentType record representing
   341	                                   the model of the referenced object
   342	
   343	            object_id (str): The primary key of the referenced object, converted
   344	                             to a string
   345	
   346	            model_path (str): The path to the field on the model of the source
   347	                              object where the reference was found
   348	
   349	            content_path (str): The path to the piece of content on the source
   350	                                object instance where the reference was found
   351	        """
   352	        # Extract references from fields
   353	        for field in object._meta.get_fields():
   354	            if field.is_relation and field.many_to_one:
   355	                if getattr(field, "wagtail_reference_index_ignore", False):
   356	                    continue
   357	
   358	                if getattr(
   359	                    field.related_model, "wagtail_reference_index_ignore", False
   360	                ):
   361	                    continue
   362	
   363	                if isinstance(field, (ParentalKey, GenericRel)):
   364	                    continue
   365	
   366	                if isinstance(field, GenericForeignKey):
   367	                    ct_field = object._meta.get_field(field.ct_field)
   368	                    fk_field = object._meta.get_field(field.fk_field)
   369	                    ct_value = ct_field.value_from_object(object)
   370	                    fk_value = fk_field.value_from_object(object)
   371	
   372	                    if ct_value is not None and fk_value is not None:
   373	                        # The content type ID referenced by the GenericForeignKey might be a subclassed
   374	                        # model, but the reference index requires us to index it under the base model's
   375	                        # content type, as that's what will be used for lookups. So, we need to convert
   376	                        # the content type back to a model class so that _get_base_content_type can
   377	                        # select the appropriate superclass if necessary, before converting back to a
   378	                        # content type.
   379	                        model = ContentType.objects.get_for_id(ct_value).model_class()
   380	                        yield (
   381	                            cls._get_base_content_type(model).id,
   382	                            str(fk_value),
   383	                            field.name,
   384	                            field.name,
   385	                        )
   386	
   387	                    continue
   388	
   389	                if isinstance(field, GenericRel):
   390	                    continue
   391	
   392	                value = field.value_from_object(object)
   393	                if value is not None:
   394	                    yield (
   395	                        cls._get_base_content_type(field.related_model).id,
   396	                        str(value),
   397	                        field.name,
   398	                        field.name,
   399	                    )
   400	
   401	            if hasattr(field, "extract_references"):
   402	                value = field.value_from_object(object)
   403	                if value is not None:
   404	                    yield from (
   405	                        (
   406	                            cls._get_base_content_type(to_model).id,
   407	                            to_object_id,
   408	                            f"{field.name}.{model_path}",
   409	                            f"{field.name}.{content_path}",
   410	                        )
   411	                        for to_model, to_object_id, model_path, content_path in field.extract_references(
   412	                            value
   413	                        )
   414	                    )
   415	
   416	        # Extract references from child relations
   417	        if isinstance(object, ClusterableModel):
   418	            for child_relation in get_all_child_relations(object):
   419	                relation_name = child_relation.get_accessor_name()

   453	    @classmethod
   454	    def create_or_update_for_object(cls, object):

   456	        Creates or updates ReferenceIndex records for the given object.
   457	
   458	        This method will extract any outbound references from the given object
   459	        and insert/update them in the database.

   468	        # uniquely define a reference

   474	        # ordered from most to least specific
   475	        content_types = [
   476	            ContentType.objects.get_for_model(model_or_object, for_concrete_model=False)
   477	            for model_or_object in ([object] + object._meta.get_parent_list())
   478	        ]

   484	        # Construct a dict mapping reference records to the (content_type_id, id) pair that the
   485	        # existing database entry is found under

   487	            (to_content_type_id, to_object_id, model_path, content_path): (
   488	                content_type_id,
   489	                id,
   490	            )
   491	            for id, content_type_id, to_content_type_id, to_object_id, model_path, content_path in cls.objects.filter(
   492	                base_content_type=base_content_type, object_id=object.pk
   493	            ).values_list(
   494	                "id",
   495	                "content_type_id",
   496	                "to_content_type",
   497	                "to_object_id",
   498	                "model_path",
   499	                "content_path",
   500	            )
   501	        }

   503	        # Construct the set of reference records that have been found on the object but are not
   504	        # already present in the database
   505	        new_references = references - set(existing_references.keys())
   506	
   507	        bulk_create_kwargs = {}
   508	        if connection.features.supports_ignore_conflicts:
   509	            bulk_create_kwargs["ignore_conflicts"] = True
   510	
   511	        # Create database records for those reference records
   512	        cls.objects.bulk_create(
   513	            [
   514	                cls(
   515	                    content_type=content_type,
   516	                    base_content_type=base_content_type,
   517	                    object_id=object.pk,
   518	                    to_content_type_id=to_content_type_id,
   519	                    to_object_id=to_object_id,
   520	                    model_path=model_path,
   521	                    content_path=content_path,
   522	                    content_path_hash=cls._get_content_path_hash(content_path),
   523	                )
   524	                for to_content_type_id, to_object_id, model_path, content_path in new_references
   525	            ],
   526	            **bulk_create_kwargs,
   527	        )

   529	        # Delete removed references
   530	        deleted_reference_ids = []

   532	        # reference in the database
   533	        for reference_data, (content_type_id, id) in existing_references.items():
   534	            if reference_data in references:
   535	                # Do not delete this reference, as it is still present in the new set
   536	                continue

   538	            if content_type_id not in known_content_type_ids:

   540	                # superclass. We can infer that the existing record is for a more specific subclass

   544	                # relation which can only be seen on the more specific model.

   547	            # If we reach here, this is a legitimate deletion - add it to the list of IDs to delete
   548	            deleted_reference_ids.append(id)

   551	        cls.objects.filter(id__in=deleted_reference_ids).delete()

   615	    @classmethod
   616	    def get_references_to_in_bulk(cls, objects):
   617	        """
   618	        Returns all inbound references for the given objects.
   619	
   620	        Args:

   622	            records for, which may be of different models
   623	
   624	        Returns:
   625	            A QuerySet of ReferenceIndex records
   626	        """
   627	        if isinstance(objects, models.QuerySet):
   628	            return cls.objects.filter(
   629	                to_content_type_id=cls._get_base_content_type(objects.model).pk,
   630	                to_object_id__in=objects.values_list(
   631	                    Cast("pk", output_field=models.CharField()),
   632	                    flat=True,
   633	                ),
   634	            )
   635	        if not objects:
   636	            return cls.objects.none()

   638	        objects_by_ct = groupby(
   639	            sorted(objects, key=lambda obj: cls._get_base_content_type(obj).pk),
   640	            key=lambda obj: cls._get_base_content_type(obj).pk,
   641	        )

   643	        for content_type_id, objs in objects_by_ct:
   644	            condition |= models.Q(
   645	                to_content_type_id=content_type_id,
   646	                to_object_id__in=[str(obj.pk) for obj in objs],
   647	            )

The stored-reference set includes several different mechanisms: real FK/OneToOne fields to `Page`, generic content-type/object-id pairs that normalize all page subclasses to base `Page`, and extracted references from rich text / chooser blocks. I’m now pinning the admin and API flows because they are numerous and tightly coupled to IDs, `content_type_id`, `.specific`, tree filters, and publication state.

The admin surface is the largest cluster, but most of it funnels through a smaller set of view/action classes. I’m treating a dependent as a production code path or schema point, not every repeated `page.id` read inside the same method; the final JSON will still carry every dependency point I found with file:line and the specific contract it touches.

    14	class CopyForm(forms.Form):
    15	    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
    16	        # CopyPage must be passed a 'page' kwarg indicating the page to be copied
    17	        self.page = kwargs.pop("page")
    18	        self.user = kwargs.pop("user")
    19	        can_publish = page_permission_policy.user_has_permission(self.user, "publish")
    20	
    21	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
    22	        self.fields["new_title"] = forms.CharField(
    23	            initial=self.page.title, label=_("New title")
    24	        )
    25	        allow_unicode = getattr(settings, "WAGTAIL_ALLOW_UNICODE_SLUGS", True)
    26	        self.fields["new_slug"] = forms.SlugField(
    27	            initial=self.page.slug,
    28	            label=_("New slug"),
    29	            allow_unicode=allow_unicode,
    30	            widget=widgets.SlugInput(locale=self.page.locale),
    31	        )
    32	        self.fields["new_parent_page"] = forms.ModelChoiceField(
    33	            initial=self.page.get_parent(),
    34	            queryset=Page.objects.all(),
    35	            widget=widgets.AdminPageChooser(
    36	                target_models=self.page.specific_class.allowed_parent_page_models(),
    37	                can_choose_root=True,
    38	                user_perms="copy_to",
    39	            ),
    40	            label=_("New parent page"),
    41	            help_text=_("This copy will be a child of this given parent page."),
    42	        )
    43	        pages_to_copy = self.page.get_descendants(inclusive=True)
    44	        subpage_count = pages_to_copy.count() - 1
    45	        if subpage_count > 0:
    46	            self.fields["copy_subpages"] = forms.BooleanField(
    47	                required=False,
    48	                initial=True,
    49	                label=_("Copy subpages"),
    50	                help_text=ngettext(
    51	                    "This will copy %(count)s subpage.",
    52	                    "This will copy %(count)s subpages.",
    53	                    subpage_count,
    54	                )
    55	                % {"count": subpage_count},
    56	            )
    57	
    58	        if can_publish:
    59	            pages_to_publish_count = pages_to_copy.live().count()
    60	            if pages_to_publish_count > 0:
    61	                # In the specific case that there are no subpages, customise the field label and help text
    62	                if subpage_count == 0:
    63	                    label = _("Publish copied page")
    64	                    help_text = _(
    65	                        "This page is live. Would you like to publish its copy as well?"
    66	                    )
    67	                else:
    68	                    label = _("Publish copies")
    69	                    help_text = ngettext(
    70	                        "%(count)s of the pages being copied is live. Would you like to publish its copy?",
    71	                        "%(count)s of the pages being copied are live. Would you like to publish their copies?",
    72	                        pages_to_publish_count,
    73	                    ) % {"count": pages_to_publish_count}
    74	
    75	                self.fields["publish_copies"] = forms.BooleanField(
    76	                    required=False, initial=False, label=label, help_text=help_text
    77	                )
    78	
    79	            # Note that only users who can publish in the new parent page can create an alias.
    80	            # This is because alias pages must always match their original page's state.
    81	            self.fields["alias"] = forms.BooleanField(
    82	                required=False,
    83	                initial=False,
    84	                label=_("Alias"),
    85	                help_text=_("Keep the new pages updated with future changes"),
    86	            )
    87	
    88	    def clean(self):
    89	        cleaned_data = super().clean()
    90	
    91	        # Make sure the slug isn't already in use
    92	        slug = cleaned_data.get("new_slug")
    93	
    94	        # New parent page given in form or parent of source, if parent_page is empty
    95	        parent_page = cleaned_data.get("new_parent_page") or self.page.get_parent()
    96	
    97	        # creating an alias requires publish permission
    98	        is_publishing = cleaned_data.get("publish_copies") or cleaned_data.get("alias")
    99	
   100	        # If the user requested to publish the copies, check they're allowed to publish at the destination
   101	        if (
   102	            is_publishing
   103	            and not parent_page.permissions_for_user(self.user).can_publish_subpage()
   104	        ):
   105	            self._errors["new_parent_page"] = self.error_class(
   106	                [
   107	                    _('You do not have permission to publish pages at "%(page_title)s"')
   108	                    % {
   109	                        "page_title": parent_page.specific_deferred.get_admin_display_title()
   110	                    }
   111	                ]
   112	            )
   113	
   114	        # check if user is allowed to copy the pages.
   115	        if not self.page.permissions_for_user(self.user).can_copy_to(parent_page):
   116	            self._errors["new_parent_page"] = self.error_class(
   117	                [
   118	                    _('You do not have permission to copy to page "%(page_title)s"')
   119	                    % {
   120	                        "page_title": parent_page.specific_deferred.get_admin_display_title()
   121	                    }
   122	                ]
   123	            )
   124	
   125	        # Count the pages with the same slug within the context of our copy's parent page
   126	        if slug and parent_page.get_children().filter(slug=slug).count():
   127	            self._errors["new_slug"] = self.error_class(
   128	                [
   129	                    _(
   130	                        'This slug is already in use within the context of its parent page "%(parent_page_title)s"'
   131	                    )
   132	                    % {"parent_page_title": parent_page}
   133	                ]
   134	            )
   135	            # The slug is no longer valid, hence remove it from cleaned_data
   136	            del cleaned_data["new_slug"]
   137	
   138	        # Don't allow recursive copies into self
   139	        if cleaned_data.get("copy_subpages") and (
   140	            self.page == parent_page or parent_page.is_descendant_of(self.page)
   141	        ):
   142	            self._errors["new_parent_page"] = self.error_class(
   143	                [_("You cannot copy a page into itself when copying subpages")]
   144	            )
   145	
   146	        return cleaned_data

   178	class WagtailAdminPageForm(WagtailAdminModelForm):
   179	    comment_notifications = forms.BooleanField(
   180	        widget=forms.CheckboxInput(), required=False
   181	    )
   182	
   183	    def __init__(
   184	        self,
   185	        data=None,
   186	        files=None,
   187	        parent_page=None,
   188	        subscription=None,
   189	        *args,
   190	        **kwargs,
   191	    ):
   192	        self.subscription = subscription
   193	
   194	        initial = kwargs.pop("initial", {})
   195	        if self.subscription:
   196	            initial["comment_notifications"] = subscription.comment_notifications
   197	
   198	        super().__init__(data, files, *args, initial=initial, **kwargs)
   199	
   200	        self.parent_page = parent_page
   201	
   202	        if not self.show_comments_toggle:
   203	            del self.fields["comment_notifications"]
   204	
   205	    @property
   206	    def show_comments_toggle(self):
   207	        return "comments" in self.__class__.formsets
   208	
   209	    def save(self, commit=True):
   210	        # Save comment notifications updates to PageSubscription
   211	        if self.show_comments_toggle and self.subscription:
   212	            self.subscription.comment_notifications = self.cleaned_data[
   213	                "comment_notifications"
   214	            ]
   215	            if commit:
   216	                self.subscription.save()
   217	
   218	        return super().save(commit=commit)
   219	
   220	    def is_valid(self):
   221	        comments = self.formsets.get("comments")
   222	        # Remove the comments formset if the management form is invalid
   223	        if comments and not comments.management_form.is_valid():
   224	            del self.formsets["comments"]
   225	        return super().is_valid()
   226	
   227	    def serialize_comments(self, user):
   228	        if comments := self.formsets.get("comments"):
   229	            data = comments.serialize(self.is_bound, user)
   230	        else:
   231	            data = {"comments": [], "user": user.pk, "authors": {}}
   232	        return data
   233	
   234	    def clean(self):
   235	        cleaned_data = super().clean()
   236	        if "slug" in self.cleaned_data:
   237	            page_slug = cleaned_data["slug"]
   238	            if not Page._slug_is_available(page_slug, self.parent_page, self.instance):
   239	                self.add_error(
   240	                    "slug",
   241	                    forms.ValidationError(
   242	                        _(
   243	                            "The slug '%(page_slug)s' is already in use within the parent page."
   244	                        )
   245	                        % {"page_slug": page_slug}
   246	                    ),
   247	                )
   248	
   249	        return cleaned_data
   250	
   251	    @property
   252	    def media(self):
   253	        media = super().media
   254	        if self.show_comments_toggle:
   255	            media += forms.Media(
   256	                js=["wagtailadmin/js/comments.js"],
   257	            )
   258	        return media

    21	class TaskChooserSearchForm(forms.Form):
    22	    q = forms.CharField(
    23	        label=gettext_lazy("Search term"), widget=forms.TextInput(), required=False
    24	    )
    25	
    26	    def __init__(self, *args, task_type_choices=None, **kwargs):
    27	        placeholder = kwargs.pop("placeholder", _("Search…"))
    28	        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
    29	        self.fields["q"].widget.attrs = {"placeholder": placeholder}
    30	
    31	        # Add task type filter if there is more than one task type option
    32	        if task_type_choices and len(task_type_choices) > 1:
    33	            self.fields["task_type"] = forms.ChoiceField(
    34	                choices=(
    35	                    # Append an "All types" choice to the beginning
    36	                    [(None, _("All types"))]
    37	                    # The task type choices that are passed in use the models as values, we need
    38	                    # to convert these to something that can be represented in HTML
    39	                    + [
    40	                        (get_model_string(model), verbose_name)
    41	                        for model, verbose_name in task_type_choices
    42	                    ]
    43	                ),
    44	                required=False,
    45	            )
    46	
    47	        # Save a mapping of task_type values back to the model that we can reference later
    48	        self.task_type_choices = {
    49	            get_model_string(model): model for model, verbose_name in task_type_choices
    50	        }
    51	
    52	    def is_searching(self):
    53	        """
    54	        Returns True if the user typed a search query
    55	        """
    56	        return self.is_valid() and bool(self.cleaned_data.get("q"))
    57	
    58	    @cached_property
    59	    def task_model(self):
    60	        """
    61	        Returns the selected task model.
    62	
    63	        This looks for the task model in the following order:
    64	         1) If there's only one task model option, return it
    65	         2) If a task model has been selected, return it
    66	         3) Return the generic Task model
    67	        """
    68	        models = list(self.task_type_choices.values())
    69	        if len(models) == 1:
    70	            return models[0]
    71	
    72	        elif self.is_valid():
    73	            model_name = self.cleaned_data.get("task_type")
    74	            if model_name and model_name in self.task_type_choices:
    75	                return self.task_type_choices[model_name]
    76	
    77	        return Task
    78	
    79	    def specific_task_model_selected(self):
    80	        return self.task_model is not Task

    83	class WorkflowPageForm(forms.ModelForm):
    84	    page = forms.ModelChoiceField(
    85	        queryset=Page.objects.all(),
    86	        widget=widgets.AdminPageChooser(target_models=[Page], can_choose_root=True),
    87	    )
    88	
    89	    class Meta:
    90	        model = WorkflowPage
    91	        fields = ["page"]
    92	
    93	    def clean(self):
    94	        page = self.cleaned_data.get("page")
    95	        try:
    96	            existing_workflow = page.workflowpage.workflow
    97	            if not self.errors and existing_workflow != self.cleaned_data["workflow"]:
    98	                # If the form has no errors, Page has an existing Workflow assigned, that Workflow is not
    99	                # the selected Workflow, and overwrite_existing is not True, add a new error. This should be used to
   100	                # trigger the confirmation message in the view. This is why this error is only added if there are no
   101	                # other errors - confirmation should be the final step.
   102	                self.add_error(
   103	                    "page",
   104	                    ValidationError(
   105	                        _(
   106	                            "This page already has workflow '%(workflow_name)s' assigned."
   107	                        )
   108	                        % {"workflow_name": existing_workflow},
   109	                        code="existing_workflow",
   110	                    ),
   111	                )
   112	        except AttributeError:
   113	            pass
   114	
   115	    def save(self, commit=False):
   116	        page = self.cleaned_data["page"]
   117	
   118	        if commit:
   119	            WorkflowPage.objects.update_or_create(
   120	                page=page,
   121	                defaults={"workflow": self.cleaned_data["workflow"]},
   122	            )

   125	class BaseWorkflowPagesFormSet(BaseFormSetMixin, forms.BaseInlineFormSet):
   126	    def clean(self):
   127	        """Checks that no two forms refer to the same page object"""
   128	        if any(self.errors):
   129	            # Don't bother validating the formset unless each form is valid on its own
   130	            return
   131	
   132	        pages = [
   133	            form.cleaned_data["page"]
   134	            for form in self.forms
   135	            # need to check for presence of 'page' in cleaned_data,
   136	            # because a completely blank form passes validation
   137	            if form not in self.deleted_forms and "page" in form.cleaned_data
   138	        ]
   139	        if len(set(pages)) != len(pages):
   140	            # pages list contains duplicates
   141	            raise forms.ValidationError(
   142	                _("You cannot assign this workflow to the same page multiple times.")
   143	            )

   180	    def __init__(self, *args, workflow=None, **kwargs):
   260	        # We go back to the object to read (in order to reapply) the
   261	        # permissions which were set on this group, but which are not
   262	        # accessible in the wagtail admin interface, as otherwise these would
   263	        # be clobbered by this form.
   264	        try:
   265	            untouchable_permissions = self.instance.permissions.exclude(
   266	                pk__in=self.registered_permissions
   267	            )
   268	            bool(
   269	                untouchable_permissions
   270	            )  # force this to be evaluated, as it's about to change
   271	        except ValueError:
   272	            # this form is not bound; we're probably creating a new group
   273	            untouchable_permissions = []
   274	        group = super().save(commit=commit)
   275	        group.permissions.add(*untouchable_permissions)
   276	        return group

   279	class PagePermissionsForm(forms.Form):
   280	    """
   281	    Note 'Permissions' (plural). A single instance of this form defines the permissions
   282	    that are assigned to an entity (i.e. group or user) for a specific page.
   283	    """
   284	
   285	    page = forms.ModelChoiceField(
   286	        queryset=Page.objects.all(),
   287	        widget=AdminPageChooser(show_edit_link=False, can_choose_root=True),
   288	    )
   289	    permissions = forms.ModelMultipleChoiceField(
   290	        queryset=Permission.objects.filter(
   291	            content_type__app_label="wagtailcore",
   292	            content_type__model="page",
   293	            codename__in=PAGE_PERMISSION_CODENAMES,
   294	        )
   295	        .select_related("content_type")
   296	        .order_by("codename"),
   297	        # Use codename as the field to use for the option values rather than pk,
   298	        # to minimise the changes needed since we moved to the Permission model
   299	        # and to ease testing.
   300	        # Django advises `to_field_name` to be a unique field. While `codename`
   301	        # is not unique by itself, it is unique together with `content_type`, so
   302	        # it is unique in the context of the above queryset.
   303	        to_field_name="codename",
   304	        required=False,
   305	        widget=forms.CheckboxSelectMultiple,
   306	    )

   309	class BaseGroupPagePermissionFormSet(BaseFormSetMixin, forms.BaseFormSet):
   310	    # defined here for easy access from templates
   311	    permission_types = PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES
   312	
   313	    def __init__(self, data=None, files=None, instance=None, prefix="page_permissions"):
   314	        if instance is None:
   315	            instance = Group()
   316	
   317	        if instance.pk is None:
   318	            full_page_permissions = []
   319	        else:
   320	            full_page_permissions = instance.page_permissions.select_related(
   321	                "page", "permission"
   322	            ).order_by("page")
   323	
   324	        self.instance = instance
   325	
   326	        initial_data = []
   327	
   328	        for page, page_permissions in groupby(
   329	            full_page_permissions,
   330	            lambda pp: pp.page,
   331	        ):
   332	            initial_data.append(
   333	                {
   334	                    "page": page,
   335	                    "permissions": [pp.permission for pp in page_permissions],
   336	                }
   337	            )
   338	
   339	        super().__init__(data, files, initial=initial_data, prefix=prefix)
   340	
   341	    def clean(self):
   342	        """Checks that no two forms refer to the same page object"""
   343	        if any(self.errors):
   344	            # Don't bother validating the formset unless each form is valid on its own
   345	            return
   346	
   347	        pages = [
   348	            form.cleaned_data["page"]
   349	            for form in self.forms
   350	            # need to check for presence of 'page' in cleaned_data,
   351	            # because a completely blank form passes validation
   352	            if form not in self.deleted_forms and "page" in form.cleaned_data
   353	        ]
   354	        if len(set(pages)) != len(pages):
   355	            # pages list contains duplicates
   356	            raise forms.ValidationError(
   357	                _("You cannot have multiple permission records for the same page.")
   358	            )
   359	
   360	    @transaction.atomic
   361	    def save(self):
   362	        if self.instance.pk is None:
   363	            raise Exception(
   364	                "Cannot save a GroupPagePermissionFormSet for an unsaved group instance"
   365	            )
   366	
   367	        # get a set of (page, permission) tuples for all ticked permissions
   368	        forms_to_save = [
   369	            form
   370	            for form in self.forms
   371	            if form not in self.deleted_forms and "page" in form.cleaned_data
   372	        ]
   373	
   374	        final_permission_records = set()
   375	        for form in forms_to_save:
   376	            for permission in form.cleaned_data["permissions"]:
   377	                final_permission_records.add((form.cleaned_data["page"], permission))
   378	
   379	        # fetch the group's existing page permission records, and from that, build a list
   380	        # of records to be created / deleted
   381	        permission_ids_to_delete = []
   382	        permission_records_to_keep = set()
   383	
   384	        for pp in self.instance.page_permissions.all():
   385	            if (pp.page, pp.permission) in final_permission_records:
   386	                permission_records_to_keep.add((pp.page, pp.permission))
   387	            else:
   388	                permission_ids_to_delete.append(pp.pk)
   389	
   390	        self.instance.page_permissions.filter(pk__in=permission_ids_to_delete).delete()
   391	
   392	        permissions_to_add = final_permission_records - permission_records_to_keep
   393	        GroupPagePermission.objects.bulk_create(
   394	            [
   395	                GroupPagePermission(
   396	                    group=self.instance,
   397	                    page=page,
   398	                    permission=permission,
   399	                )
   400	                for (page, permission) in permissions_to_add
   401	            ]
   402	        )
   403	
   404	    def as_admin_panel(self):
   405	        return render_to_string(
   406	            "wagtailusers/groups/includes/page_permissions_formset.html",
   407	            {"formset": self},
   408	        )
   409

    84	    def old_links(self, site_root_paths=None):

    88	        :param site_root_paths: Pre-calculated root paths (obtained from `Site.get_site_root_paths`)

    91	        if self.site_id is not None:

   112	    @staticmethod
   113	    def add_redirect(
   114	        old_path,
   115	        redirect_to=None,
   116	        is_permanent=True,
   117	        page_route_path=None,
   118	        site=None,
   119	        automatically_created=False,
   120	    ):
   121	        """
   122	        Create and save a Redirect instance with a single method.
   123	
   124	        :param old_path: the path you wish to redirect
   125	        :param site: the Site (instance) the redirect is applicable to (if not all sites)
   126	        :param redirect_to: a Page (instance) or path (string) where the redirect should point
   127	        :param is_permanent: whether the redirect should be indicated as permanent (i.e. 301 redirect)
   128	        :return: Redirect instance
   129	        """
   130	        redirect = Redirect()
   131	
   132	        # Set redirect properties from input parameters
   133	        redirect.old_path = Redirect.normalise_path(old_path)
   134	        redirect.site = site
   135	
   136	        # Check whether redirect to is string or Page
   137	        if isinstance(redirect_to, Page):
   138	            # Set redirect page
   139	            redirect.redirect_page = redirect_to
   140	            # Set redirect page route
   141	            if isinstance(page_route_path, str):
   142	                redirect.redirect_page_route_path = Redirect.normalise_page_route_path(
   143	                    page_route_path
   144	                )
   145	        elif isinstance(redirect_to, str):
   146	            # Set redirect link string
   147	            redirect.redirect_link = redirect_to
   148	
   149	        redirect.is_permanent = is_permanent
   150	        redirect.automatically_created = automatically_created
   151	
   152	        redirect.save()
   153	
   154	        return redirect

   156	    @staticmethod
   157	    def normalise_path(url, decode_unicode=True):
   158	        """
   159	        Normalize a URL path for storage or lookup on the Redirect model. All paths that normalize to the
   160	        same value are considered equivalent when looking up redirects.
   161	
   162	        :param url: The URL to normalize
   163	        :param decode_unicode: Whether to decode percent-encoded characters to unicode. This is a necessary step
   164	            for true normalization, but we allow it to be skipped to allow our lookup code to try both encoded and
   165	            decoded forms without a redundant round-trip to re-encode the path.
   166	        :return: The normalized path
   167	        """
   168	        # Strip whitespace
   169	        url = url.strip()
   170	
   171	        # Parse url
   172	        url_parsed = urlparse(url)
   173	
   174	        # Path must start with / but not end with /
   175	        path = url_parsed.path
   176	        if not path.startswith("/"):
   177	            path = "/" + path
   178	
   179	        if path.endswith("/") and len(path) > 1:
   180	            path = path[:-1]
   181	
   182	        # Parameters must be sorted alphabetically
   183	        parameters = url_parsed.params
   184	        parameters_components = parameters.split(";")
   185	        parameters = ";".join(sorted(parameters_components))
   186	
   187	        # Query string components must be sorted alphabetically
   188	        query_string = url_parsed.query
   189	        query_string_components = query_string.split("&")
   190	        query_string = "&".join(sorted(query_string_components))
   191	
   192	        if parameters:
   193	            path = path + ";" + parameters
   194	
   195	        # Add query string to path
   196	        if query_string:
   197	            path = path + "?" + query_string
   198	
   199	        # Reverse url-encoding so that unicode characters are written
   200	        # to the database as such
   201	        if decode_unicode:
   202	            path = uri_to_iri(path)
   203	
   204	        return path
   205	
   206	    @staticmethod
   207	    def normalise_page_route_path(url):
   208	        # Strip whitespace
   209	        url = url.strip()
   210	        if not url:
   211	            return ""
   212	
   213	        # Extract the path from the rest of the value
   214	        path = urlparse(url).path
   215	
   216	        if path == "/":
   217	            return ""
   218	        elif not path.startswith("/"):
   219	            path = "/" + path

   151	    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
   152	        """

   154	        JSON storage reference for this field. Previously created fields will be updated
   155	        to use the legacy unidecode method via checks & _migrate_legacy_clean_name.
   156	        We do not want to update the clean name on any subsequent changes to the label
   157	        as this would invalidate any previously submitted data.
   158	        """
   159	
   160	        is_new = self.pk is None
   161	        if is_new:
   162	            clean_name = self.get_field_clean_name()
   163	            self.clean_name = clean_name

   122	    """
   123	    Purge a single page from the frontend cache.
   124	
   125	    :param page: The page to purge from the cache.
   126	    :type page: Page
   127	    :param backend_settings: Optional custom backend settings to use instead of those defined in ``settings.WAGTAILFRONTENDCACHE``.
   128	    :type backend_settings: dict, optional

   130	    :type backends: list, optional

   132	    :type cache_object: object, optional
   133	
   134	    This function retrieves all cached URLs for the given page and purges them from the configured
   135	    backends. It's useful when you need to invalidate the cache for a specific page,
   136	    for example after the page has been updated.
   137	
   138	    If no custom backends or settings are provided, it will use the default configuration
   139	    from ``settings.WAGTAILFRONTENDCACHE``.
   140	
   141	    The `cache_object` parameter can be any kind of object that supports arbitrary attribute
   142	    assignment, such as a Python object or Django Model instance.
   143	    """
   144	    urls = _get_page_cached_urls(page, cache_object)
   145	    purge_urls_from_cache(urls, backend_settings, backends)

   149	    pages, backend_settings=None, backends=None, *, cache_object=None
   150	):
   151	    """
   152	    Purge multiple pages from the frontend cache.
   153	
   154	    :param pages: An iterable of pages to purge from the cache.
   155	    :type pages: iterable of Page
   156	    :param backend_settings: Optional custom backend settings to use instead of those defined in ``settings.WAGTAILFRONTENDCACHE``.
   157	    :type backend_settings: dict, optional
   158	    :param backends: Optional list of strings matching keys from ``settings.WAGTAILFRONTENDCACHE`` or provided as ``backend_settings``. Can be used to limit purge operations to specific backends.
   159	    :type backends: list, optional
   160	    :param cache_object: Optional object to be passed to URL-related methods, to allow cached site root path data to be reused across multiple requests to this method. If not provided, the ``PurgeBatch`` object created by this method will be used instead.
   161	    :type cache_object: object, optional
   162	
   163	    This function retrieves all cached URLs for the given pages and purges them from the configured
   164	    backends. It's useful when you need to invalidate the cache for multiple pages at once,
   165	    for example after a bulk update operation.
   166	
   167	    If no custom backends or settings are provided, it will use the default configuration
   168	    from ``settings.WAGTAILFRONTENDCACHE``.
   169	
   170	    The `cache_object` parameter can be any kind of object that supports arbitrary attribute
   171	    assignment, such as a Python object or Django Model instance.
   172	    """
   173	    batch = PurgeBatch(cache_object=cache_object)
   174	    batch.add_pages(pages)
   175	    batch.purge(backend_settings, backends)

   178	class PurgeBatch:
   179	    """Represents a list of URLs to be purged in a single request"""
   180	
   181	    def __init__(self, urls=None, *, cache_object=None):
   182	        self.urls = set()
   183	
   184	        if urls is not None:
   185	            self.add_urls(urls)
   186	
   187	        self.cache_object = cache_object
   188	
   189	    def add_url(self, url):
   190	        """Adds a single URL"""
   191	        self.urls.add(url)
   192	
   193	    def add_urls(self, urls):
   194	        """
   195	        Adds multiple URLs from an iterable
   196	
   197	        This is equivalent to running ``.add_url(url)`` on each URL
   198	        individually
   199	        """
   200	        self.urls.update(urls)
   201	
   202	    def add_page(self, page):
   203	        """
   204	        Adds all URLs for the specified page
   205	
   206	        This combines the page's full URL with each path that is returned by
   207	        the page's `.get_cached_paths` method
   208	        """
   209	        self.add_urls(_get_page_cached_urls(page, self.cache_object or self))
   210	
   211	    def add_pages(self, pages):
   212	        """
   213	        Adds multiple pages from a QuerySet or an iterable
   214	
   215	        This is equivalent to running ``.add_page(page)`` on each page
   216	        individually
   217	        """
   218	        for page in pages:
   219	            self.add_page(page)
   220	
   221	    def purge(self, backend_settings=None, backends=None):
   222	        """
   223	        Performs the purge of all the URLs in this batch
   224	
   225	        This method takes two optional keyword arguments: backend_settings and backends
   226	
   227	        - backend_settings can be used to override the WAGTAILFRONTENDCACHE setting for
   228	          just this call
   229	
   230	        - backends can be set to a list of backend names. When set, the invalidation request
   231	          will only be sent to these backends
   232	        """
   233	        purge_urls_from_cache(self.urls, backend_settings, backends)

     4	# which causes it to be loaded early in startup if wagtail.contrib.sitemaps is

     9	class Sitemap(DjangoSitemap):
    10	    def __init__(self, request=None):
    11	        self.request = request
    12	
    13	    def location(self, obj):
    14	        return obj.get_full_url(self.request)
    15	
    16	    def lastmod(self, obj):
    17	        # fall back on latest_revision_created_at if last_published_at is null
    18	        # (for backwards compatibility from before last_published_at was added)
    19	        return obj.last_published_at or obj.latest_revision_created_at
    20	
    21	    def get_wagtail_site(self):
    22	        from wagtail.models import Site
    23	
    24	        site = Site.find_for_request(self.request)
    25	        if site is None:
    26	            return Site.objects.select_related("root_page").get(is_default_site=True)
    27	        return site
    28	
    29	    def items(self):
    30	        return (
    31	            self.get_wagtail_site()
    32	            .root_page.get_descendants(inclusive=True)
    33	            .live()
    34	            .public()
    35	            .order_by("path")
    36	            .defer_streamfields()
    37	            .specific()
    38	        )
    39	
    40	    def _urls(self, page, protocol, domain):
    41	        urls = []
    42	        last_mods = set()
    43	
    44	        for item in self.paginator.page(page).object_list.iterator():
    45	            url_info_items = item.get_sitemap_urls(self.request)
    46	
    47	            for url_info in url_info_items:
    48	                urls.append(url_info)
    49	                last_mods.add(url_info.get("lastmod"))
    50	
    51	        # last_mods might be empty if the whole site is private
    52	        if last_mods and None not in last_mods:
    53	            self.latest_lastmod = max(last_mods)
    54	        return urls

    49	class RoutablePageMixin:
    50	    """
    51	    This class can be mixed in to a Page model, allowing extra routes to be
    52	    added to it.
    53	    """
    54	
    55	    @path("")
    56	    def index_route(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
    57	        request.is_preview = getattr(request, "is_preview", False)
    58	
    59	        return TemplateResponse(
    60	            request,
    61	            self.get_template(request, *args, **kwargs),
    62	            self.get_context(request, *args, **kwargs),
    63	        )
    64	
    65	    @classmethod
    66	    def get_subpage_urls(cls):
    67	        routes = []
    68	
    69	        # Loop over this class's defined routes, in method resolution order.
    70	        # Routes defined in the immediate class take precedence, followed by
    71	        # immediate superclass and so on
    72	        for klass in cls.__mro__:
    73	            routes_for_class = []
    74	            for val in klass.__dict__.values():
    75	                if hasattr(val, "_routablepage_routes"):
    76	                    routes_for_class.extend(val._routablepage_routes)
    77	
    78	            # sort routes by _creation_counter so that ones earlier in the class definition
    79	            # take precedence
    80	            routes_for_class.sort(key=lambda route: route[1])
    81	
    82	            routes.extend(route[0] for route in routes_for_class)
    83	
    84	        return tuple(routes)
    85	
    86	    @classmethod
    87	    def get_resolver(cls):
    88	        if "_routablepage_urlresolver" not in cls.__dict__:
    89	            subpage_urls = cls.get_subpage_urls()
    90	            cls._routablepage_urlresolver = URLResolver(
    91	                RegexPattern(r"^/"), subpage_urls
    92	            )
    93	
    94	        return cls._routablepage_urlresolver
    95	
    96	    @classmethod
    97	    def check(cls, **kwargs):
    98	        errors = super().check(**kwargs)
    99	        errors.extend(cls._check_path_with_regex())
   100	        return errors
   101	
   102	    @classmethod
   103	    def _check_path_with_regex(cls):
   104	        routes = cls.get_subpage_urls()
   105	        errors = []
   106	        for route in routes:
   107	            if isinstance(route.pattern, RoutePattern):
   108	                pattern = route.pattern._route
   109	                if (
   110	                    "(?P<" in pattern
   111	                    or pattern.startswith("^")
   112	                    or pattern.endswith("$")
   113	                ):
   114	                    errors.append(
   115	                        Warning(
   116	                            (
   117	                                f"Your URL pattern {route.name or route.callback.__name__} has a "
   118	                                "route that contains '(?P<', begins with a '^', or ends with a '$'."
   119	                            ),
   120	                            hint="Decorate your view with re_path if you want to use regexp.",
   121	                            obj=cls,
   122	                            id="wagtailroutablepage.W001",
   123	                        )
   124	                    )
   125	        return errors
   126	
   127	    def reverse_subpage(self, name, args=None, kwargs=None):
   128	        """
   129	        This method takes a route name/arguments and returns a URL path.
   130	        """
   131	        args = args or []
   132	        kwargs = kwargs or {}
   133	
   134	        return self.get_resolver().reverse(name, *args, **kwargs)
   135	
   136	    def resolve_subpage(self, path):
   137	        """
   138	        This method takes a URL path and finds the view to call.
   139	        """
   140	        resolver_match = self.get_resolver().resolve(path)
   141	
   142	        # Bind the method
   143	        resolver_match.func = resolver_match.func.__get__(self, type(self))
   144	
   145	        return resolver_match
   146	
   147	    def route(self, request, path_components):
   148	        """
   149	        This hooks the subpage URLs into Wagtail's routing.
   150	        """
   151	        if self.live:
   152	            try:
   153	                path = "/"
   154	                if path_components:
   155	                    path += "/".join(path_components) + "/"
   156	
   157	                resolver_match = self.resolve_subpage(path)
   158	                request.routable_resolver_match = resolver_match
   159	                view, args, kwargs = resolver_match
   160	                return RouteResult(self, args=(view, args, kwargs))
   161	            except Http404:
   162	                pass
   163	
   164	        return super().route(request, path_components)
   165	
   166	    def serve(self, request, view=None, args=None, kwargs=None):
   167	        if args is None:
   168	            args = []
   169	        if kwargs is None:
   170	            kwargs = {}
   171	        if view is None:
   172	            return super().serve(request, *args, **kwargs)
   173	        return view(request, *args, **kwargs)
   174	
   175	    def render(self, request, *args, template=None, context_overrides=None, **kwargs):
   176	        """
   177	        This method replicates what ``Page.serve()`` usually does when ``RoutablePageMixin``
   178	        is not used. By default, ``Page.get_template()`` is called to derive the template
   179	        to use for rendering, and ``Page.get_context()`` is always called to gather the
   180	        data to be included in the context.
   181	
   182	        You can use the ``context_overrides`` keyword argument as a shortcut to override or
   183	        add new values to the context. For example:
   184	
   185	        .. code-block:: python
   186	
   187	            @path('') # override the default route
   188	            def upcoming_events(self, request):
   189	                return self.render(request, context_overrides={
   190	                    'title': "Current events",
   191	                    'events': EventPage.objects.live().future(),
   192	                })
   193	
   194	        You can also use the ``template`` argument to specify an alternative
   195	        template to use for rendering. For example:
   196	
   197	        .. code-block:: python
   198	
   199	            @path('past/')
   200	            def past_events(self, request):
   201	                return self.render(
   202	                    request,
   203	                    context_overrides={
   204	                        'title': "Past events",
   205	                        'events': EventPage.objects.live().past(),
   206	                    },
   207	                    template="events/event_index_historical.html",
   208	                )
   209	        """
   210	        if template is None:
   211	            template = self.get_template(request, *args, **kwargs)
   212	        context = self.get_context(request, *args, **kwargs)
   213	        context.update(context_overrides or {})
   214	        return TemplateResponse(request, template, context)
   215	
   216	    def serve_preview(self, request, mode_name):
   217	        view, args, kwargs = self.resolve_subpage("/")
   218	        return view(request, *args, **kwargs)

    60	def can_choose_page(
    61	    page,
    62	    user,
    63	    desired_classes,
    64	    can_choose_root=True,
    65	    user_perm=None,
    66	    target_pages=None,
    67	    match_subclass=True,
    68	):
    69	    """Returns boolean indicating of the user can choose page.
    70	    will check if the root page can be selected and if user permissions
    71	    should be checked.
    72	    """
    73	
    74	    if not target_pages:
    75	        target_pages = []
    76	
    77	    if not match_subclass and page.specific_class not in desired_classes:
    78	        return False
    79	    elif (
    80	        match_subclass
    81	        and not issubclass(page.specific_class or Page, desired_classes)
    82	        and not desired_classes == (Page,)
    83	    ):
    84	        return False
    85	    elif not can_choose_root and page.is_root():
    86	        return False
    87	
    88	    if user_perm in ["move_to", "bulk_move_to"]:
    89	        pages_to_move = target_pages
    90	
    91	        for page_to_move in pages_to_move:
    92	            if page.pk == page_to_move.pk or page.is_descendant_of(page_to_move):
    93	                return False
    94	
    95	            if user_perm == "move_to":
    96	                return page_to_move.permissions_for_user(user).can_move_to(page)
    97	    if user_perm in {"add_subpage", "copy_to"}:
    98	        return page.permissions_for_user(user).can_add_subpage()
    99	
   100	    return True

   524	        ]
   525	        return self.render_chosen_response(result)

      "wagtail/models/pages.py:1726 copy delegates to CopyPageAction",
      "wagtail/models/pages.py:1760 create_alias delegates to CreatePageAliasAction",
      "wagtail/models/pages.py:1801 permissions_for_user returns PagePermissionTester",
      "wagtail/models/pages.py:1896 get_ancestors queries Page.objects.ancestor_of",
      "wagtail/models/pages.py:1903 get_descendants queries Page.objects.descendant_of",
      "wagtail/models/pages.py:1910 get_siblings queries Page.objects.sibling_of",
      "wagtail/models/pages.py:1925 get_view_restrictions uses ancestor pages and alias_of",
      "wagtail/models/pages.py:1981 with_content_json preserves id, content_type, path, depth, numchild, publication state, owner, locks, revisions, locale, alias",
      "wagtail/models/pages.py:2074 has_workflow checks WorkflowPage on ancestors",
      "wagtail/models/pages.py:2111 natural_key is url_path",

    "reference_contract_notes": [
      "Direct page references are usually FK/OneToOne fields to base `wagtailcore.Page`, not specific page subclasses.",
      "Polymorphic page identity is stored through `Page.content_type`; query code often calls `.specific()` or `.specific_deferred` before reading custom fields.",
      "Tree queries are coupled to `path`, `depth`, `numchild`, `steplen`, and treebeard methods such as `get_parent`, `get_children`, `is_descendant_of`, `add_child`, and `move`.",
      "Public routing is coupled to `Site.root_page`, `url_path`, `slug`, `.route()`, `.serve()`, and `live`.",
      "Publishing state is split across base page fields and generic Revision/WorkflowState rows keyed by base Page content type plus object_id.",
      "ReferenceIndex, rich text, chooser blocks, revisions, logs, workflows, and APIs normalize specific page subclasses back to base Page content type."
    ]
  },
